subject: Voter survey: Fraud concerns
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www
body:
Voter Action Project [https://secure.everyaction.com/Qyv_-_BiF0iduOUt5sZ0yw2?am=25.00&contactdata=HzAW+Dr/IMGiRRCMP6SalX5m41gGwXHRocU9PJQRyqpnXhFO5tFyUvB7arKDOXA5QeO6fHw6g4RMLSdAo0kI8FJ8wPwhXNSmvnbB4hXj3h4=]
WaPo - The truth about election fraud: It’s rare [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/EA/EA007/1/89215/images/WaPo_TruthElectionFraudRare.png]
As recent elections got underway, many Americans, in particular those who identify as Republicans, were increasingly fearful that the outcome would be affected or even determined by election fraud.
Thousands of poll watchers were dispatched. Drop boxes were under surveillance. A Fox News poll conducted recently still found that 55 percent of those surveyed were extremely or very concerned about voter fraud despite lacking any evidence of fraud. Among self-identified Republicans, nearly three-quarters said they were that concerned.
Team: Are you worried about voter fraud?
By clicking a response below your answer will be automatically recorded:

Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.

body:

| This is a personal message for you. We are reaching out because your name is still missing from our petition files.

As a top Democrat in your state, we thought you would be one of the first to sign on in support of the new lawsuit against Elon Musk and DOGE.

_**Was this a mistake?**_

It’s not too late to sign, but with this lawsuit unfolding quickly, we need to know where you stand ASAP: [**Add your name to sign our petition now! »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/sue-musk?source=VPP_20250413_EMPET_E5_T0_NS&t=6&akid=19526%2E878179%2EGsoHmA) | [CO-SPONSOR THE LAWSUIT AGAINST ELON MUSK »](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/sue-musk?source=VPP_20250413_EMPET_E5_T0_NS&t=7&akid=19526%2E878179%2EGsoHmA)

**BREAKING: Elon Musk Sued Over Social Security Cuts**
[**CALLING ON 15,000 DEMOCRATS: SIGN ON TO CO-SPONSOR THE LAWSUIT AGAINST MUSK »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/sue-musk?source=VPP_20250413_EMPET_E5_T0_NS&t=8&akid=19526%2E878179%2EGsoHmA) [](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/sue-musk?source=VPP_20250413_EMPET_E5_T0_NS&t=9&akid=19526%2E878179%2EGsoHmA) | [ADD MY NAME TO THE PETITION »](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/sue-musk?source=VPP_20250413_EMPET_E5_T0_NS&t=10&akid=19526%2E878179%2EGsoHmA)
---
**FIRST ➤** Unelected billionaire Elon Musk SEIZED control of the federal government — even YOUR SOCIAL SECURITY NUMBER!

**THEN ➤** Musk refused to back down, threatening to HALT Social Security payments if he didn’t get unfettered access to our private data!

**SO NOW ➤** The American people are taking a stand, Musk is now being SUED for this disgusting attack. _AMAZING!_
_So we’re calling on 15,000 of our MOST dedicated Democrats to stand with this lawsuit and DEMAND Elon Musk be held accountable!_
[**CALLING ON 15,000 DEMOCRATS: SIGN ON TO CO-SPONSOR THE LAWSUIT AGAINST MUSK »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/sue-musk?source=VPP_20250413_EMPET_E5_T0_NS&t=11&akid=19526%2E878179%2EGsoHmA) [](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/sue-musk?source=VPP_20250413_EMPET_E5_T0_NS&t=12&akid=19526%2E878179%2EGsoHmA) | [ADD MY NAME TO THE PETITION »](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/sue-musk?source=VPP_20250413_EMPET_E5_T0_NS&t=13&akid=19526%2E878179%2EGsoHmA)

The Voter Protection Project is on an important mission: To end partisan gerrymandering and voter suppression by electing candidates at the state and federal levels that are forceful advocates for voting rights. | | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/mansharamani/p/the-anxiety-before-college-selection?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/893b0ce5-b6a8-4d77-8a2c-2058db6746f2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
I recently wrote about the anxiety each Spring brings to students (and their parents) as they struggle to make a college selection that seemingly sets the course of their entire lives. My advice: Relax...it's not about where one goes as much as what one does once on campus. Where or whether to go to college may seem to be a monumental decision, but it probably doesn't matter as much as most people think.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/e6db2e7a-3b94-4aad-b915-f10525549932?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)| | |
#### [College Selection Anxiety?](https://substack.com/redirect/e6db2e7a-3b94-4aad-b915-f10525549932?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
---
| | [Vikram Mansharamani](https://substack.com/profile/37278065-vikram-mansharamani)| | *
---|---|---
| Apr 5
| [| Read full story
---](https://substack.com/redirect/e6db2e7a-3b94-4aad-b915-f10525549932?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
The extraordinarily successful people I have known in my life have been everything from high school dropouts to the holders of multiple PhDs. Wherever you go, whatever you do, there will be chances to learn that will set your life's trajectory; it's what you do when presented with these opportunities that really matters. And the college application process that gets students to this point can be even more agonizing for them and their parents.
Unsurprisingly, securing a seat at an elite university is a goal most American college-bound students seek, as it is supposedly a surefire means to a life of status and success. Parents start the process early, trying to give their kids every conceivable advantage: advanced placement classes, SAT prep courses, community service, college essay practice, etc. Some parents even encourage their kids to focus on less competitive sports to gain an edge. [Others](https://substack.com/redirect/330b3d19-74b7-4670-a05d-3f0111890346?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) go so far as to [hire](https://substack.com/redirect/07a3b9ca-3dc4-4bc0-951a-8dbb732cfbb0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) "college application consultants" to package their kids as more attractive prospects at costs of up to [several hundred thousand dollars](https://substack.com/redirect/ce592046-2d4d-47e2-aa8e-a3702715dd3c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). And some are willing to go much, much further.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..D-q-SUa5xgfTgSt806Lsiccd75-w8c2wn7yWxpt0h3w?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=161266937)
In 2019, [the "Varsity Blues" college admissions scandal](https://substack.com/redirect/b3a8f864-a718-4245-8f4e-901882a0d6b8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) exposed some of the scheming and scams, shattering the notion that our university system was a ladder of meritocracy. The allegations included bribing admissions officers, paying for others to take SATs, faking disabilities to get more time taking tests, and buying off coaches to have applicants falsely labeled as elite recruited athletes. One student was called an African-American tennis star, even though he was white and didn't play tennis; another's face was photoshopped onto a picture of someone else playing a sport the student had never actually played.
Lately, as the children of my friends and colleagues grow and approach college age, I am often asked about my experiences and if there is any advice I might have on how to apply and get into a top school. I tell them all the same thing:
_**There 's no set formula.**_
I say this based on lots of experience with students in elite schools. I personally graduated from Yale and MIT; I taught at Yale and Harvard. I have served as an alumni interviewer for decades. I have seen hundreds if not thousands of the students who have been admitted to these elite institutions. I have carefully observed the admissions process, and I have ruminated on the qualities, skills, traits, and backgrounds of those who got in and those who did not. And here's my conclusion as to how the "best of the best" are selected: _**There is no secret sauce, there aren 't any magic words, there's no way to honestly influence the process.**_
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/29ed5529-76b0-4e9a-82d1-b033aa5886ce?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Photo by [Priscilla Du Preez 🇨🇦](https://substack.com/redirect/e1808195-29b1-4e59-ba5d-a301547729cd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) on [Unsplash](https://substack.com/redirect/36076403-68dd-4d7b-81d6-978b5c4561d8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Sure, it takes a lot of hard work and intelligence to get into the pool of qualified candidates. And yes, some might be the children of alumni (i.e. "legacies") or star athletes, which might give them an "edge," but from there the process may be more random than you might think.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..D-q-SUa5xgfTgSt806Lsiccd75-w8c2wn7yWxpt0h3w?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=161266937)
As David McCullough Jr. - a high school teacher and son of historian David McCullough Sr. - noted during a commencement address at Wellesley High school, there are 37,000 high schools in the United States and that means there are, "37,000 valedictorians...37,000 class presidents...92,000 harmonizing altos...340,000 swaggering jocks..." No student is special, he concludes, because everyone is special. For anyone who hasn't seen his speech, I've pasted a link below. It's well worth the time.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/e93ed648-d66f-4b86-acd6-ca0b2b8096f2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
So, my **advice for parents** is simple: Take a deep breath and relax. Sure, push your kids to do well but also give them the tools they will need to succeed in life, don't just check boxes for the admissions process. Let your kids enjoy their childhood and encourage them to enjoy college. And of course, savor your time with them while they are under your roof; once kids leave the nest, most will learn to fly and may never fully return.
And my **advice for students** : Be genuine, work hard, and do your best. Volunteer to help the homeless, not to impress an admissions officer. Run track not to become a recruited athlete, but because you love running. Read and study not for the sake of a grade but for to acquire new knowledge. Doing so will generate fulfillment from service, physical habits that keep you healthy, and intellectual capacity to thrive in an ever-changing world. **Focus on the journey, not the destination, because the journey is all there is. And in the end, we are all destined for the same place.**
[ Upgrade to paid](https://substack..D-q-SUa5xgfTgSt806Lsiccd75-w8c2wn7yWxpt0h3w?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=161266937)
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1661999&post_id=161266937&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTYxMjY2OTM3LCJpYXQiOjE3NDQ1OTI0ODksImV4cCI6MTc0NzE4NDQ4OSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE2NjE5OTkiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.H_7aIO1ny8LX5jiK-9HQbYxb0FOceJTjgbC0CbDV1z8)

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/69bae6b1-e7c0-4e0f-b461-fa2ac9b706a6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
**VIKRAM MANSHARAMANI** is an entrepreneur, consultant, scholar, neighbor, husband, father, volunteer, and professional generalist who thinks in multiple-dimensions and looks beyond the short-term. Self-taught to think around corners and connect original dots, he spends his time speaking with global leaders in business, government, academia, and journalism. He's currently the Chairman and CEO of Goodwell Foods, a manufacturer of private label frozen pizza. _LinkedIn_ has twice listed him as its #1 Top Voice in Money & Finance, and _Worth_ profiled him as one of the 100 Most Powerful People in Global Finance. Vikram earned a PhD From MIT, has taught at Yale and Harvard, and is the author of three books, _**[The Making of a Generalist: An Independent Thinker Finds Unconventional Success in an Uncertain World](https://substack.com/redirect/fcbfb3fd-994f-473a-a151-909de3e2ca93?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_ , _**[Think for Yourself: Restoring Common Sense in an Age of Experts and Artificial Intelligence](https://substack.com/redirect/fa91f6b0-f4d6-4c77-be5a-70a7d398148e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_ and _**[Boombustology: Spotting Financial Bubbles Before They Burst](https://substack.com/redirect/070098e4-dc7d-44e1-a1d7-c1bba91eb6f2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_. Vikram lives in Lincoln, New Hampshire with his wife and two children, where they can usually be found hiking or skiing.

clean_body: Focus on the Journey, Not the Destination. | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more ? Focus on the Journey, Not the Destination. | | | Apr 14 | | | | | | | | | | | | | | ---|---|--- I recently wrote about the anxiety each Spring brings to students (and their parents) as they struggle to make a college selection that seemingly sets the course of their entire lives. My advice: Relax...it's not about where one goes as much as what one does once on campus. Where or whether to go to college may seem to be a monumental decision, but it probably doesn't matter as much as most people think. | | | | | | | ---|---|--- | Apr 5 | The extraordinarily successful people I have known in my life have been everything from high school dropouts to the holders of multiple PhDs. Wherever you go, whatever you do, there will be chances to learn that will set your life's trajectory; it's what you do when presented with these opportunities that really matters. And the college application process that gets students to this point can be even more agonizing for them and their parents. Unsurprisingly, securing a seat at an elite university is a goal most American college-bound students seek, as it is supposedly a surefire means to a life of status and success. Parents start the process early, trying to give their kids every conceivable advantage: advanced placement classes, SAT prep courses, community service, college essay practice, etc. Some parents even encourage their kids to focus on less competitive sports to gain an edge. go so far as to "college application consultants" to package their kids as more attractive prospects at costs of up to . And some are willing to go much, much further. In 2019, exposed some of the scheming and scams, shattering the notion that our university system was a ladder of meritocracy. The allegations included bribing admissions officers, paying for others to take SATs, faking disabilities to get more time taking tests, and buying off coaches to have applicants falsely labeled as elite recruited athletes. One student was called an African-American tennis star, even though he was white and didn't play tennis; another's face was photoshopped onto a picture of someone else playing a sport the student had never actually played. Lately, as the children of my friends and colleagues grow and approach college age, I am often asked about my experiences and if there is any advice I might have on how to apply and get into a top school. I tell them all the same thing: There 's no set formula. I say this based on lots of experience with students in elite schools. I personally graduated from Yale and MIT; I taught at Yale and Harvard. I have served as an alumni interviewer for decades. I have seen hundreds if not thousands of the students who have been admitted to these elite institutions. I have carefully observed the admissions process, and I have ruminated on the qualities, skills, traits, and backgrounds of those who got in and those who did not. And here's my conclusion as to how the "best of the best" are selected: There is no secret sauce, there aren 't any magic words, there's no way to honestly influence the process. | | ---|---|--- Photo by on Sure, it takes a lot of hard work and intelligence to get into the pool of qualified candidates. And yes, some might be the children of alumni (i.e. "legacies") or star athletes, which might give them an "edge," but from there the process may be more random than you might think. As David McCullough Jr. - a high school teacher and son of historian David McCullough Sr. - noted during a commencement address at Wellesley High school, there are 37,000 high schools in the United States and that means there are, "37,000 valedictorians...37,000 class presidents...92,000 harmonizing altos...340,000 swaggering jocks..." No student is special, he concludes, because everyone is special. For anyone who hasn't seen his speech, I've pasted a link below. It's well worth the time. So, my advice for parents is simple: Take a deep breath and relax. Sure, push your kids to do well but also give them the tools they will need to succeed in life, don't just check boxes for the admissions process. Let your kids enjoy their childhood and encourage them to enjoy college. And of course, savor your time with them while they are under your roof; once kids leave the nest, most will learn to fly and may never fully return. And my advice for students : Be genuine, work hard, and do your best. Volunteer to help the homeless, not to impress an admissions officer. Run track not to become a recruited athlete, but because you love running. Read and study not for the sake of a grade but for to acquire new knowledge. Doing so will generate fulfillment from service, physical habits that keep you healthy, and intellectual capacity to thrive in an ever-changing world. Focus on the journey, not the destination, because the journey is all there is. And in the end, we are all destined for the same place. | | ---|---|--- VIKRAM MANSHARAMANI is an entrepreneur, consultant, scholar, neighbor, husband, father, volunteer, and professional generalist who thinks in multiple-dimensions and looks beyond the short-term. Self-taught to think around corners and connect original dots, he spends his time speaking with global leaders in business, government, academia, and journalism. He's currently the Chairman and CEO of Goodwell Foods, a manufacturer of private label frozen pizza. LinkedIn has twice listed him as its #1 Top Voice in Money & Finance, and Worth profiled him as one of the 100 Most Powerful People in Global Finance. Vikram earned a PhD From MIT, has taught at Yale and Harvard, and is the author of three books, , and **. Vikram lives in Lincoln, New Hampshire with his wife and two children, where they can usually be found hiking or skiing. Peter, we’ve just received breaking news out of Florida:
Governor Ron DeSantis has officially called on the Secretary of State to schedule TWO special elections on a RAPIDLY accelerated timeline.
To put it simply: Democrats now have a limited time to prepare to win TWO MORE HOUSE SEATS being vacated by Trump cabinet appointments
These special elections will determine the future of FL-01 , vacated by Matt Gaetz (reportedly stepping up as Trump’s pick for Attorney General), and FL-06 , soon to be left by Mike Waltz (rumored to become Trump’s National Security Advisor).
Flipping these suits blue IS possible - but only if we start preparing NOW. Will you help us build up a warchest of funding to rush to the Democrats who run for these seats? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-floridaspecials?refcode=111724_1]

Chip in NOW so we can have a stash of funds ready to go when Democrats put up a candidate for these seats >>> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-floridaspecials?refcode=111724_1]

name: Jena Griswold
subject: From Estes Park to this moment
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jena for Colorado
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2402-q2-jg-launch?refcode=250413-emailpm [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2402-q2-jg-launch?refcode=250413-emailpm]
Peter,
I want to take a moment to share a little bit about who I am and why I’m running to be Colorado’s next Attorney General. Before I go into that, can you chip in $10 to help power this campaign? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2402-q2-jg-launch?refcode=250413-emailpm&amount=10]
I grew up in a working-class family in Estes Park. At times, we relied on food stamps to get by, but my mom worked hard and taught me the value of perseverance and hard work. I became the first person in my family to graduate from a four-year college, and eventually, I earned my law degree from the University of Pennsylvania Law School.
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2402-q2-jg-launch?refcode=250413-emailpm [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2402-q2-jg-launch?refcode=250413-emailpm]
Public service has always been my purpose. From protecting voting rights to helping secure hundreds of millions of relief dollars to help Colorado communities rebuild after the 2013 floods, I’ve dedicated my career to standing up for others. As Secretary of State, I fought to expand voting rights, protect election workers, and stand strong against extremists—even in the face of threats.
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2402-q2-jg-launch?refcode=250413-emailpm [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2402-q2-jg-launch?refcode=250413-emailpm]
Now I’m ready to take that fight to the Attorney General’s office. I’ll work every day to protect all Coloradans’ rights and freedoms, and will stand up to corporate special interests that take advantage of working people. I’ll never back down to extremists, and I'll fight for fairness and justice—making sure victims of crime are supported and our communities are safer and stronger.
I’m proud of where I come from and the path that brought me here. And I’m ready to continue fighting for the people of Colorado.
Will you join me on this journey? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2402-q2-jg-launch?refcode=250413-emailpm]
Thank you for believing in this movement,
Jena Griswold
Follow Jena Online:
https://www.facebook.com/Jenaforcolorado/ [https://www.facebook.com/Jenaforcolorado/] https://twitter.com/JenaGriswold [https://twitter.com/JenaGriswold] https://www.instagram.com/jenagriswold/ [https://www.instagram.com/jenagriswold/] https://www.threads.net/@jenagriswold [https://www.threads.net/@jenagriswold] https://bsky.app/profile/jenagriswold.bsky.social [https://bsky.app/profile/jenagriswold.bsky.social]
Paid for by Jena for Colorado. Rachel Gordon, Registered Agent.

Louisville, CO 80027
United States

Independent election analysts at Cook Political have determined that California officially has more vulnerable blue districts than ANY other state. We aren’t waiting until 2026 to start defending our vulnerable Democrats – if we want to flip the U.S. House, we have to start the work of protecting those seats NOW. = = = Cook Political is a premier election forecaster that identifies the most competitive swing seats nationwide. And they’ve determined that 5 members of California’s Congressional delegation – including 4 new members who we successfully elected in 2024 – will have the most competitive re-election efforts in 2026. In 2024, we flipped three of these districts from red to blue by hiring organizers and opening regional offices earlier than ever — in many cases over a year before Election Day. That was only possible because of people like you, folks. = = = = = = Thanks, \- CADEM | | = Our Democratic leaders are working hard to make California a better place to live. #
[Alexandria just met with 20,000 folks in Salt Lake City, Utah. The arena was packed! We want to share what she said tonight (which is below). But first, if you’re able to, will you chip in to help us host more town halls across the country?](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20250413.LWsnXA&refcode=em20250414-8604-P_FND_RES_GRM&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=8604%2E2526796%2E5AgPvN)
[](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20250413.LWsnXA&refcode=em20250414-8604-P_FND_RES_GRM&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=2&akid=8604%2E2526796%2E5AgPvN)
What's up, Utah? How are we doing tonight? Are you all ready to change the world? Are you all ready to change this country? Good, because it starts in places like Salt Lake City. Thank you. It is incredible, incredible to be here tonight with you all. You know, I love you too.
We're actually seeing that our numbers tonight, it looks like we've got 20,000 people here tonight. We've got 5,000 people outside who just want to be part of this moment. So thank you, thank you, thank you all so much.
And I want to thank each and every one of you for taking and making the effort of being here today, for taking the time out of your days in order to show up here tonight, so thank you.
My name is Alexandria. Some of you may know me as AOC.
And we are all here together because we share the frustration and heartache that comes from watching those in power actively tear down or refuse to fight for everyday, working Americans like us.
And we're here because an extreme concentration of power, greed, and corruption is taking over this country like never before. Oligarchy in America.
And we must acknowledge the terrifying moment that we are in right now, and that what we are hearing and seeing with our own eyes is in fact happening.
We are watching as our neighbors, students, and friends are being fired, targeted, and disappeared. It is real.
People we love are being targeted and harassed for being LGBTQ. Our co-workers, U.S. citizens, and immigrants alike are being disappeared off the street.
Educators are being fired for teaching American history accurately. And activists are being detained with no charge or evidence for using their First Amendment rights, especially if they are using them to try to end the war in Gaza.
In fact, the Trump administration admits that it has jailed a young father and husband, Mahmoud Khalil, without any evidence or charge of a crime.
And they admit that they have thrown him in a cell thousands of miles away from his family in New York because he attended a protest and are detaining him for the content of his speech and nothing more than that.
But let's be clear, Utah.
Donald Trump's detention of Mahmoud Khalil for his speech is anti-American. And we demand his release, along with the release of Rümeysa Öztürk, who he is also jailing for writing an op-ed in her school paper.
And we demand this, Utah, because we do have power in this moment.
All of us do, you do, and everyday people do.
And it will never be just institutions and officials alone that uphold our democracy, it will always be the people, the masses, who refuse to comply with authoritarian regimes, who are the last and strongest defense of our country and our freedoms.
Because we know that a better world is possible, and we are willing to do something about it.
But to get there, we must be honest about how we got to where we are right now. Because this moment didn't come out of nowhere.
It has been a long time coming.
The destruction of our rights and our democracy is directly tied to the growing and extreme wealth inequality that has been building in America for years.
It is not a coincidence that billionaires like Elon Musk dumped billions into this election to elect Donald Trump.
And he sure isn't doing it out of charity. For years, we have known that our political system has slowly but surely become dominated by big money and billionaires. And time after time, we've seen how our government and laws are more responsive to lobbyists than to the will of everyday people.
And we also know that the agenda of dark money to keep wages low and loot our public goods to give to the rich is deeply unpopular with the people of all parties.
But just look at what Republicans have been quietly doing in Congress, voting to advance cuts on hundreds of billions of dollars from Medicaid and veterans benefits, so that they can take that money and give it to the billionaires in the form of tax cuts and sweetheart deals to companies like SpaceX.
And you know who from Utah voted for that, by the way? Both of your senators, Mike Lee and John Curtis. And Salt Lake City's own House members, Blake Moore and Celeste Maloy. And they know, Utah, that that's not what you want.
They know that it is deeply unpopular. They know that it hurts working families from Utah. But they know that they are not there to serve the working class. They are there to serve themselves and the billionaires who paid them.
But they know, they also know, that the only chance they have to get away with such an unpopular agenda is to stoke deep divisions along race, identity, and culture to keep us fighting and distracted from one another.
And this has been the big money playbook not just now, not just today, but it has been for decades. And that is why Donald Trump is not in aberration.
He is the logical, inevitable conclusion of an American political system dominated by corporate and dark money. And if we are to defeat him, Utah, we must defeat the system that created him.
We are at a crossroads in America. We can either have extreme and growing wealth inequality with the toxic division and corruption that it requires to survive, or we can have a fair economy for working people along with a democracy and freedoms that uphold it.
Oligarchy or democracy, but we cannot have both.
I grew up in a working-class family. My mom cleaned houses and my dad worked to build a small business. My parents worked themselves to the bone so that my brother and I could just have a shot, the American dream.
And for a moment there, it looked like we were going to make it. We had a home, bills were getting a little less stressful to pay, and I was doing well in school. And what may have looked like a modest life to others was a dream come true for us.
But suddenly, when I was a teenager, my dad was diagnosed with a rare form of cancer.
And after a long fight, we lost him. And he actually passed away in September of 2008, just as the economy crashed in the Great Recession and millions of jobs across this country vanished.
It felt like the world of our family fell apart as the larger world was falling apart too.
And overnight, my mom was left to figure out how to provide for two kids by herself, put them through school, keep our house, pay off my dad's medical bills, all on a house cleaner salary.
And that's how I became a waitress. Because it was millions of innocent working families who paid the price, while the billionaires and thieves who defrauded our financial system got off richer than ever before.
And not a single one of them went to jail.
And you know what, Utah?
That is the story that Republicans mock. Not mine, but all of ours, when they say a waitress or working person is unqualified to serve in Congress.
But the fact is, many of us are far more qualified to know what real life is actually like than any of them ever will.
And I tell this story not because it is special, but because of how common and normal it is.
While the details may differ, so many of us know what it feels like for life to be one bad day, one bad piece of news, one major setback from everything feeling like it's going to fall apart.
And we don't have to live like this anymore, Utah.
We can make a new world, a better country, where we can fight for the dignity of all people.
And it looks like living wages, Utah.
It looks like stable housing, Utah.
It looks like guaranteed health care, Salt Lake City.
And it looks like respect for all of our differences, no matter who we are or where we come from.
Oligarchy or democracy. And we are here today because we choose democracy. We choose freedom.
And that means that we choose to out-organize the oligarchy. We will do away, and we must do away, with the power of big money.
And that is why, even as a waitress, with a long shot campaign, I decided that day that I would never take money from lobbyists or corporations, and I never will.
And when I got to Congress, I was genuinely shocked by how the place worked. Even having run against dark money in the first place, the everyday influence of corruption was astonishing.
We have somehow conditioned ourselves to believe that it is normal for members of Congress who swore an oath to the people to somehow day-trade individual stocks and make millions with the sensitive information we are entrusted with to serve.
How, I ask you, can anyone possibly make an objective vote on health care, energy, or war when their personal money is tied up in pharmaceutical companies, oil companies, or defense companies' stocks? You can't.
And we saw this play out this week with Trump's corrupt and disastrous tariff scheme.
And I hope that we can see now that Trump's tariff scheme didn't have anything to do with manufacturing like they claimed—it was all about manipulating the markets so he could quietly enrich his friends who bought the tip before reversing it all in the morning. All while our seniors and retirees suffered.
Salt Lake City, this is a matter of fact.
Donald Trump is a criminal. He was found guilty of 34 felony counts. Of course he is lying and manipulating the stock market too.
He makes himself rich, the billionaires who back him rich, and the members of Congress who collude with him rich, but not you or us or any working person in this country.
And to be clear, I don't care what party you are, Democrat or Republican, it doesn't matter how powerful or what positions you have, members of Congress holding and trading individual stock is wrong. It is corrosive, it is destructive, and it must be banned.
And you can run the tape—because I have always held this position whether it was Democrats or Republicans in charge.
And I'll give you another example of something that's shocking when I got to Congress. Just this year, I was named to the Energy and Commerce Committee, this is one of the most powerful committees in all of Congress.
And for years, lobbyists fought to keep me off it. In no small part because I believe in Medicare for All. And because lobbyists don't like what they don't control.
And an interesting thing happened the day that it was announced that I was put on that committee. Our phones and the phones in my office rang off the hook, our inboxes exploded with emails from virtually every corporate lobbyist under the sun.
All of a sudden, they wanted to be friends. But you know what, Salt Lake City?
Because of you all, because of your support, because you all show up, because you allow me to not take a dime in corporate money, you free me to say no.
And we can free others to say no too. I've seen how these groups bully members of Congress: They threaten to take money away or threaten to spend it against them if they don't do what they want. And what they want is for our lives to be as expensive as possible on the lowest possible wage.
And it trickles down to how life feels for working people today.
But I want us to understand that this constant pressure that we feel in our lives, of the water rising up to our throats, the impossibility to afford anything easily, the fear of speaking up or being who we are in public, the bitter divisions driven more by online algorithms than the truth, the crumbling of our rights and protections, understand that this, right now, is what it feels like to be governed by billionaires.
This is what oligarchy feels like. Not someday, right now. The first step is for us to open our eyes.
Because, of course, it is a system that depends on division to enable inequality that will result in a president who is best at dividing us and most skilled at corruption.
And it will also reward the Republicans in Congress who support that agenda, no matter what crimes are committed.
Big money is how we got Donald Trump.
His whole presidency began with launching tools for bribery, meme coins and cryptocurrencies to fleece normal people and move money in the dark, extortion settlements for media companies and law firms who gladly paid the price, removing duly elected officials from the inauguration, even Republican ones, to make room for the unelected billionaires who sat right behind him.
They have one central economic mission: to make billionaires richer at the expense of the United States of America.
And it does not surprise us that their first economic mission has been to target Medicaid, Medicare, Social Security, firing our federal workers and cutting benefits for our veterans for hundreds of billions of dollars so they can hand that money off to the wealthiest.
And Utah, I know that it can look or feel impossible sometimes, for the Republicans to be defeated.
But that is not true. It is simply not true.
From the waitress who is now speaking to you today, I can tell you: impossible is nothing. Miracles start with the faith of mustard seeds. And that is what each and every one of you represents today. Small miracles of faith in ourselves, in each other, and in the refusal to give up.
Because we are here for the long haul, Utah. We are here for the school boards and the community associations, for the small business groups and town councils, for public art and gestures of inclusion, and we will build from there until we put the seats in Congress, until we take back the Senate right here in this very state.
So you know what that means. We start working now, we start working small, and we will defeat Celeste Maloy and Blake Moore, and give them the boot, and we will replace them with a brawling Democrat who fights for the working class.
They are out there, maybe you're right here in this stadium today.
Because this, Utah, is the path to guaranteeing healthcare to every American, to establish a living wage, to tackle skyrocketing rents and mortgages, to take on the climate crisis, and establish a country where the American dream is possible for all of us.
So I will end where I began. We might all come from different places, but we share many of the same experiences.
So to all of those who came here today, unsure of whether or not this is where you belong, I want to say that you do.
No matter if you have all the right words to say, no matter your race, religion, gender identity, or status, no matter even if you disagree with me on a couple of things, if you are willing to fight for someone you don't know, you are welcome here.
If you are willing to fight for working people, regardless of who they are, you are welcome here.
Because here, everyone is valued. But, we have to stand together.
We cannot be tempted to turn on our neighbors, or be fooled into thinking that we really are that much different than a trans or queer kid who just wants to be accepted.
We can't be fooled into thinking that we're that much different than a dreamer who strives for a better future and to fulfill the American dream.
Hate is a trap that sinks us all.
And it is standing together—without exception, to reject division—that is the only way that we can win.
So I hope you see that this movement is not about partisan labels or purity tests, but it is about class solidarity. It's about you, the thousands of you who came out today to stand together and say our lives deserve dignity and our work deserves respect.
And so we are here today to make a sacred promise to one another, and that is to take care of each other on our worst days and to share in the successes of our best.
Thank you, Salt Lake City. And now, it is my honor to introduce a friend, a companion, someone who has worked so hard to bring us all together and make today possible. Everyone, please put your hands together for Senator Bernie Sanders.
[Will you donate right now to help us host more town halls and power our fight on the road? These town halls take major resources to host safely.](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20250413.LWsnXA&refcode=em20250414-8604-P_FND_RES_GRM&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=3&akid=8604%2E2526796%2E5AgPvN)

And now, it is my honor to introduce a friend, a companion, someone who has worked so hard to bring us all together and make today possible. Everyone, please put your hands together for Senator Bernie Sanders. Every donation helps us mobilize, organize, and fight for the working class in the places that need it most. Pa’lante, Alexandria | ---|--- | | ---|---|--- Email us: mailto: speak@ocasiocortez.com

name: Combat Veteran Troy Downing
subject: My mission hasn’t changed
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Troy Downing for Congress
body:
I’m no longer in the service, but I’m still on a mission.

| | | [](https://trk.emails.troydowning.)
---
Hi, Peter, I didn’t come to Washington for the so-called perks. I don't need fancy cocktails – heck, I distill my own whiskey. I came here to serve, just like I did when I wore the uniform.I’m a combat veteran who’s now serving Montana in Congress. And my mission hasn’t changed: _**protect this country from those who want to destroy it from the inside out.**_ That mission is on the line. We’re days away from the end-of-quarter deadline—and I’m still short of the goal we need to defend the House and stop the radical Left.[**Can you chip in any amount today to help us close the gap?**](https://trk.emails.troydowning._oE4vlxEQv6-uEx2grqFyw)

name: House GOP
subject: Elise Stefanik’s opponent is a disaster.
body:
Blake Gendebien absolutely can not win.

| | | | [](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/ajIh/?__vbtrk=Mjg0OTQ0OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=Mjg0OTQ0OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)
---
Friend, when President Trump asked Elise to serve as U.S. Ambassador to the United Nations she dropped everything and got ready to step up.
And when it became clear later that she was needed more in the House than in the Cabinet she dropped everything again to go back and start fighting harder than ever for Agenda 47.
**Now she’s in the fight to stop the Left from clawing their way back to power.**
That fight starts with blocking the radical Leftists trying to buy this seat for a fraud named** __ Blake Gendebien**.**__[](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/ajIi/?__vbtrk=Mjg0OTQ0OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=Mjg0OTQ0OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)__**
[](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/ajIh/?__vbtrk=Mjg0OTQ0OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=Mjg0OTQ0OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)
_Blake Gendebien
Radical Open-Borders Democrat_** __[](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/ajIi/?__vbtrk=Mjg0OTQ0OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=Mjg0OTQ0OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)__**
[KEEP GENDEBIEN OUT OF CONGRESS](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/ajIh/?__vbtrk=Mjg0OTQ0OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=Mjg0OTQ0OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)
Blake Gendebien**hired illegal aliens instead of American citizens** to work his farm.
****
**When one got arrested by ICE****he shelled out $10,000 for his bail** and put that criminal right back on our streets.
He wasn’t ashamed, he was proud of it, we only know it happened because he bragged about it!
When asked about why he only hires illegal aliens -_and refuses to hire Americans_ \- he called our own people “ _awful_ ” and said we have “ _alcohol problems_ ” and “ _domestic violence issues_ ”.
**He said that American workers have no “ _practical independence_ ” or “ _ability to think_ ”.**
Yes that is a _****_**** direct quote and yes he was talking about all of us.
Friend, I can not tell you how sickened and disgusted we are to hear any person say that about Americans but to hear it from somebody that wants to serve in Congress is so disgusting that we almost can’t believe it.**__[](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/ajIi/?__vbtrk=Mjg0OTQ0OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=Mjg0OTQ0OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)__**
[KEEP GENDEBIEN OUT OF CONGRESS](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/ajIh/?__vbtrk=Mjg0OTQ0OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=Mjg0OTQ0OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)
He’s on record saying that all the money he pays illegal workers gets funneled back to their home countries...**_and he laughed about it!_**
This man wants to represent the people of NY-21 in Congress, __**[we can't let that happen](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/ajIh/?__vbtrk=Mjg0OTQ0OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=Mjg0OTQ0OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)**_**.**_
Blake Gendebien doesn’t want to serve this district,_he wants to buy it_.
He’s bankrolled by _out-of-state elites_ ,_special interests_ , and _Leftist billionaires_.
**_$3,000,000 was funneled to his campaign in just three months!_****_That’s how badly they want him in Congress._**
They know he’ll do their bidding while sneering at the people he claims to represent.
Here’s the difference between Blake Gendebien and Elise Stefanik:
**_Blake gave up $10,000 to protect an _illegal alien_..._****_Elise gave up serving in the United Nations to serve _you_!_****__[](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/ajIi/?__vbtrk=Mjg0OTQ0OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=Mjg0OTQ0OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)__**
[KEEP GENDEBIEN OUT OF CONGRESS](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/ajIh/?__vbtrk=Mjg0OTQ0OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=Mjg0OTQ0OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)
When Elise was set to leave the House and become Ambassador to the United Nations she couldn’t fundraise the way she needed to stand up to a well funded candidate like Blake Gendebien.
In fact, she had to _**stop fundraising for nearly five full months**_.
Now, she’s being _**outspent**_ , _**outgunned**_ , and _**flooded with lies**_.
**__[Everything is on the line so we’re asking patriots like you, if you’re able, to join this fight today and pitch in whatever you can to help Elise Stefanik defeat Blake Gendebien](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/ajIh/?__vbtrk=Mjg0OTQ0OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=Mjg0OTQ0OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)_._**
We can’t let a Radical Democrat like Blake Gendebien get a seat in Congress. Not _now,_ not _ever._ With your support we will secure victory.
_For America,_
**House GOP**
[](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/ajIh/?__vbtrk=Mjg0OTQ0OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=Mjg0OTQ0OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)

name: The Flyover
subject: McIlroy Wins, Suppressed Covid Report, and Casa Orgánica
body:
[Rory McIlroy won the 2025 Masters](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0jzFvwyAQRn8NbI7gOGx3YOjiLV07WmdzJFTYWEAd5d9Xyd75k7733vd1vlJMcb990cauhTyDAtsp7DTOnmJ6zii9C8zARrLTA2IPvVZW3t2qB2MDKaU8I5AlXPvRwkJ24I8wBhnd602hRq0U4nBZxqCN8WgsLr0dQaBiH1sul58c93bnkJ755HJZ8yaTu7d2VGE-BUwCJjp2ftTXJGBK8WQB00a1cand27quuXCXmDyXJVPx3e_hqXGVxfnjEVOKtR8EqttGMb0Z7b_m08FfAAAA___yv1ik) in a dramatic sudden-death playoff over Justin Rose, clinching his first green jacket and completing the career Grand Slam.

| | |
---
| | |
# Monday, April 14, 2025
---|---
**Good Morning!** On this day in 1865, [President Abraham Lincoln](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx00L1u7CAQxfGnwR0WH4O9t6C4jbtNm9IaG1hmg40FaC2_fbRR2tQ_6ejo_3mf70iJ9scHbt62kGcllOECuITZIaVrhs7Z4L3yuvNWjgCDGqQwXbQirKOG0YSAKG9ey2EdjNALovtnwggd2feaAAlSCICxX25Bau1AG1gGc1MMhHfUcumfmfYWfUhXfvnSr3nrko2tHZXp_0xNTE3nefaRasvlejtTU4tUucOL085_hakJj0KJS2Bqeua485PSl698yblFXmPOrXJcCkbceKJ9zWnvinXHSSlRHUYG4rEhpZ8T7a8oL6u-AwAA___MMGVP) was shot at Ford's Theatre by actor John Wilkes Booth, who shouted, "Sic semper tyrannis!" as he fled on horseback. Lincoln died the next morning. Today we're diving into one of the most [electrifying Masters finales](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0jzFvwyAQRn8NbI7gOGx3YOjiLV07WmdzJFTYWEAd5d9Xyd75k7733vd1vlJMcb990cauhTyDAtsp7DTOnmJ6zii9C8zARrLTA2IPvVZW3t2qB2MDKaU8I5AlXPvRwkJ24I8wBhnd602hRq0U4nBZxqCN8WgsLr0dQaBiH1sul58c93bnkJ755HJZ8yaTu7d2VGE-BUwCJjp2ftTXJGBK8WQB00a1cand27quuXCXmDyXJVPx3e_hqXGVxfnjEVOKtR8EqttGMb0Z7b_m08FfAAAA___yv1ik) in recent memory--Rory McIlroy's clutch playoff win, a long-overdue green jacket, and a storybook ending that etched his name into golf history. We're sponsored today by [Hear.com](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kDGPszAMhn-N2UCJbQIMGT4JsbXTJ92IIAklp0AQ5Kj670-080227EfW4_fr1t8GH_z6uA-L02mKPQosc8G55N4OPrx6zqyenENHmdOyYlaopCizWU9KkWyMrGtjqBoNjbW1VlJZV4hWTZnX1zXBkqUQzFUx1pMkskwlj6qsEVg461Pci-_o1zS7Kbzi6fbCxCULek5pO4D-AXaA3bD54ulC2H5S4SNgd0rAzixAnVm8BWqRm4brpZkOOW4roEqfsagA1SGB2hPzHRvRYJlvHzo310_XHoHa_7MDLFF0H493fx-Sj-sQLoaA2qsyUJvt2m5PH4I_VAUsHsvgw9s8_ZXkqfE3AAD__2IIa-0), offering affordable hearing aids with crystal-clear sound.
|
---
## Arson Suspect Held in Pennsylvania Gov. Fire
A man has been arrested after allegedly [setting fire to Pennsylvania Governor](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx00LHO4yAQBOCnsTsiFq8xLiiuSZdrr7SIdx1vRMAClChvf_Lf_-VopE-j-XdbbkGipMff8GLftrwYbUalUQEuFCR-F-zJb8yGh549TIjWWNBjv3ugMPNs0WlH1oELBBqAkTdLYO6mF39qGgFBa8TpcncbDAPhMOLdjs50qJmk5XJ5Zklt5y1-85vLZc2vPvq9taN2w5_OXDtzDUfiTz2rM5Qma-TOXJ-57qru4ZCSVSg1J3XkKCurTQqrg1Oq3_gOSYJ6nHjKRRmCiVerV3TbREgwghmIgtWrc3ae--Lp-EiMUu3UoX68gsSfVe23l97e_A8AAP__BRhoxQ) Josh Shapiro's official residence early Sunday morning, forcing the governor, his family, and guests to evacuate. Authorities say the suspect, 38-year-old Cody Balmer, faces [charges including attempted murder, terrorism, and arson](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx00DGPhCAUBOBfgx0G8KFuQXGN3V57pUF56NuwsAFOs__-4uXa675kkslkvu7z3VKguH3aJ5rq06yE0lwAlzA7S-E9Q-OMR1TYNWjkANCrXgrd7Aa9dbq_SbxJP-K4WC_QieEmUDuN_dCQudoESJBCAAztMnrZdQ46DUuvR8VAoKOacvtIFOuOPrzTgbld07MJZq_1VVj3wdTE1HSeZxuXNeJZrpyp6SJT03fhf9rSwR-p7Lzs9kU58YyFHMYVuc0lRU7xwFJps5VS5HmNVgkpAJps3OukEKj0AwOxPS2F3xX1v1cOo34CAAD__5uoZbc). The blaze caused significant damage, though no one was injured. [See photos here](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kL2q4zAQRp9G7hQkefyTQsXC4i7bbmkm1sjWImuMJBL89osDl1vd7sCBM8P39zE_MMSQ1j-4k62eZ6NMJxVIDbPDEM8ZGmc9kaG2IasHgN70WnXNZsmMmrzChRD6-x39ggv4QS1qGTU61wR71RRo0EoBDLfn6HXbOmg7ePbdaAQocqFyvv3jkOpGPp78onxbeG-i3Wo9imh_CTMJM0VeMBqd6F0uL8x04ZcQZloxRsrnN8lj48pFlo3f0uGOK0nHiWRleaBcr1OJc5E7phI4SfSVsiTM8ZQ75xTSKjEXTqKdPi3R_tZNtu54hxhD6QcBat0xxM_H9acFX9b8DwAA__9Q1HZJ). The attack occurred just hours after Shapiro [shared Passover greetings](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kb2SnDAQhJ9GZLc1GmkEBASuW2Mn58SBQ2pWP4tcgCihPXxv74K1MzuSavpTS9368Ta8cZzicv_Gs-9KSAMC0gvoF6kHx3H6GHTluuA9elX5TtZaGzQSqBq70DCClAgExkFraqvaltCxapw2DFXsDjfQUksArevLrQlSKacV6ZuhBoUG72JJ-fIzxaWMPkwf6d3ni01zNXVjKesm1CeBvcD-1zEV2H85iCXl7yOvMSeB_Va4PDaBvWyllHWrFCI1BmqtW6H67MOwZSvUtezHikSfS9gFUv1adsvz-hzt3hc_37z7oxSf51P5l-0TuecTYIabCyypVg6cIs1oyTlHITgwyI1ksqGBv1em5Ty2ycFKEGiOFz7yJNT1GRnpCE1HbMI-hsyzvyw3u_h9e7ZA2H8FeDUNHnA_bGPaLa8lntbqWvLD87qee1nlzq17nKa4mVpouM8cp7Pj8r8_f-_wdwAAAP__KgWg6g) from a family Seder at the mansion.
## iPhones Spared 145% Tariff, Still Face 20%
President Trump clarified Sunday that [Chinese-made smartphones and laptops](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx00L3u4yAQBPCnwd1GgLGdf0Fxjbtce6WFYYlJ-LBgnchvf_L1V4_0G838eSwPE2LIz98moSZfFsnlAFyBUIszIZ6L6pz2iBL7DrWYlBrlKPjQbfqHW6FW9PIuvJjcwK037sf1I1-HSRrsgr40roQSnCs13da7F33vVD-odRzukimOLlCpt1cJmTb08SwfrDdbUhf1RrQ31v9icmZyzudeGl0Rk_MFMzlzxeQseibno0HGb2NyDvtWMjYw2UGhDSvYLWRsCMk4BIxoqZYcbINviBFieGM8YUVox_pCS0AFUqkIVI-0A5kavG9gS0pYLUJDW5FMPSEelMMbmjmv4q5qt19kaOPEFH8mE-K_KfS_az9a_g0AAP__Zjx9lw), including iPhones, will not face the full 145% tariff previously feared, but they will still be hit with a 20% fentanyl-related duty. He added that those products are being reclassified under a new "tariff bucket," with future levies likely tied to a broader national security review of the semiconductor supply chain. Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick said the goal is to reduce reliance on China and bring tech manufacturing back to the U.S. See the [White House announcement here](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kL3OnDAQRZ_GdEb2MPx8hYs0dJs2JTL2eJnIYGS8sPv2EdunHM3RvVfnz2N6WI68PX_blUwJaQIFrVQoNU7ecvxMWHkTiICaiozuETvotGqrxQw42wYGp234saHHhjCQmrWbg9Y_2Fds7jSFGrVSiH09D0E3jcemxblrBxCoyHNJuf6beCsLhfhJJ-XapbWKZillP0TzS8AoYLyuq74WLrSk10H1M50Cxj3TwZ62wjZK6wqn7RAw3r0CRoUCRhdt5sDO3k-ZgqS3o_1LytfmKUt6k3sVPkmmfN8aoe1v0u6ZowT5lWIPaVfaPHkBY5WN3y-OkY-uF6ieq-X4nV3-p_E08C8AAP__Iu90wg).
## McIlroy Clinches Grand Slam at 2025 Masters
[Rory McIlroy won the 2025 Masters](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0jzFvwyAQRn8NbI7gOGx3YOjiLV07WmdzJFTYWEAd5d9Xyd75k7733vd1vlJMcb990cauhTyDAtsp7DTOnmJ6zii9C8zARrLTA2IPvVZW3t2qB2MDKaU8I5AlXPvRwkJ24I8wBhnd602hRq0U4nBZxqCN8WgsLr0dQaBiH1sul58c93bnkJ755HJZ8yaTu7d2VGE-BUwCJjp2ftTXJGBK8WQB00a1cand27quuXCXmDyXJVPx3e_hqXGVxfnjEVOKtR8EqttGMb0Z7b_m08FfAAAA___yv1ik) on Sunday in a dramatic sudden-death playoff over Justin Rose, clinching his first green jacket and completing the career Grand Slam. After a rollercoaster back nine, McIlroy delivered a clutch wedge shot to within 4 feet of the first playoff hole and sank the birdie putt for his fifth major title. Off the course, [he said watching _Bridgerton_](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kLFuhDAMhp8mbKDEmABDhi5s17UjCsRA7gI-hfROvH0FqtSpi_1L_mT589etv1kf_DZ_2pVMmrgHCVUuMVfYO-vD0WPmzEQEVGZkVI2oQStZZYtxw1SObVlLPWCrGg16HO2kqlFaLRWqzJtzm0SFSkrEuhiaSZWlw7LCQVcNCJTkfOJY3NlvaaEpHPyiWIy8ZsEsKT13UX4I6AR0M4fpTfQovneb2NnjpAR0e-J4nP3JMe2_oIButXeO-xX2RFc6zxHQSTxL8zfKL-vI8cjX0YfIR54WyiOND978NudD9G6mmHgT0DXQNm0LlZS1gC6Lxj3fPgS_61qgnFfrwyWQ_nvoy8BPAAAA__8U-XU8) with his wife and _Zootopia_ with his daughter helped keep him relaxed. Despite a [28% drop in Thursday viewership](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kLFuwyAUAL8Gb1iAH3Y6MHTxlq4dLTAP-1UYLCCx8vdVunc_3Un3fV_uliKl7cseaFrIixJKcwFcwuItxdcCnTcBUeHQoZETwKhGKXS3mw-7DtrdQEg36cmiC3p0uHqlrXBaqY7M2yZAghQCYOrdLchh8DBocKO-KQYCPbVc-p9Mqe0Y4is_sfRrPrpo9tbOyoZPpmamZnuFR7Qp5UdaKW1vhqkZ65mYmg9bG5bKA5XaeMmP5HmxjdJWuS_55I02LPzK2dd-b0fsivHnRTFSHScGYjssxb9u--_D06jfAAAA__8ukGHC), the finale at Augusta National Golf Club in Augusta, Georgia, delivered a thrilling ending that captivated golf fans around the world.

clean_body: in a dramatic sudden-death playoff over Justin Rose, clinching his first green jacket and completing the career Grand Slam. | | | | | | Monday, April 14, 2025 ---|--- Good Morning! On this day in 1865, was shot at Ford's Theatre by actor John Wilkes Booth, who shouted, "Sic semper tyrannis!" as he fled on horseback. Lincoln died the next morning. Today we're diving into one of the most in recent memory--Rory McIlroy's clutch playoff win, a long-overdue green jacket, and a storybook ending that etched his name into golf history. We're sponsored today by , offering affordable hearing aids with crystal-clear sound. | Arson Suspect Held in Pennsylvania Gov. Fire A man has been arrested after allegedly Josh Shapiro's official residence early Sunday morning, forcing the governor, his family, and guests to evacuate. Authorities say the suspect, 38-year-old Cody Balmer, faces . The blaze caused significant damage, though no one was injured. . The attack occurred just hours after Shapiro from a family Seder at the mansion. iPhones Spared 145% Tariff, Still Face 20% President Trump clarified Sunday that , including iPhones, will not face the full 145% tariff previously feared, but they will still be hit with a 20% fentanyl-related duty. He added that those products are being reclassified under a new "tariff bucket," with future levies likely tied to a broader national security review of the semiconductor supply chain. Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick said the goal is to reduce reliance on China and bring tech manufacturing back to the U.S. See the . McIlroy Clinches Grand Slam at 2025 Masters on Sunday in a dramatic sudden-death playoff over Justin Rose, clinching his first green jacket and completing the career Grand Slam. After a rollercoaster back nine, McIlroy delivered a clutch wedge shot to within 4 feet of the first playoff hole and sank the birdie putt for his fifth major title. Off the course, with his wife and Zootopia with his daughter helped keep him relaxed. Despite a , the finale at Augusta National Golf Club in Augusta, Georgia, delivered a thrilling ending that captivated golf fans around the world. name: Team Vivek
subject: Don’t open this if you love paying taxes
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY VIVEK RAMASWAMY FOR OHIO
body:
Paying your income tax tomorrow might be unavoidable — but if Vivek is elected Governor, it could be one of the last times you ever do.

[](https://trk.emails.dailygopnews._cmVjdXJyaW5nPXRydWUmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPTIwMjUwNDE0X1VHLTIxLjExNDUxMF90MTYyNjU2OC0zNjIzJmV4X3RpZD0yMDI1MDQxNF9VRy0yMS4xMTQ1MTBfdDE2MjY1NjgtMzYyMyJ9/5wbGwFXfRr7bcgMtOOfnrQ)REMINDER: TAXES ARE DUE TOMORROW 4/15The government takes your paycheck, wastes your money, and then demands even more.
**Vivek Ramaswamy says: **[Enough.](https://trk.emails.dailygopnews._cmVjdXJyaW5nPXRydWUmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPTIwMjUwNDE0X1VHLTIxLjExNDUxMF90MTYyNjU2OC0zNjIzJmV4X3RpZD0yMDI1MDQxNF9VRy0yMS4xMTQ1MTBfdDE2MjY1NjgtMzYyMyJ9/B6BNyVSSTnpe2TZ5V8li9Q)
If elected Governor, Vivek will lead Ohio to ELIMINATE the state income tax!
Texas and Florida are thriving without one--and Ohio can, too. Working families deserve a break. Not another hand in their pocket.
Paying your income tax tomorrow might be unavoidable -- but if Vivek is elected Governor, it could be one of the last times you ever do.

name: History Facts
subject: What did people do before bathrooms?
body:
As difficult as it is to imagine, access to a full bathroom wasn’t a household norm until well into the 19th century.
[](https://track.historyfacts.com/?xtl=1krv9cfc68epj7py2q8mf9t2zo1ob2yxqm0iw4dx6q84o0e58y3wjsv7bxwjwn1dmjlmm9dp2kdbea48xuqa3icjyld4ouq5bdogx5o1nyh32iqg9mgzgwytfj4wglgnxzx0smnnj8nq602aurpup4dj5&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=supe2r&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)
---
---
# [Bathrooms didn’t exist until the 19th century.](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=supe2r&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)
### [Science & Industry](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=supe2r&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)
[](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=supe2r&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)
| A s difficult as it is to imagine, access to a full bathroom wasn’t a household norm until [well into the 19th century](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=supe2r&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com). Though the flush toilet was invented in England in 1596, the general public still used chamber pots and outhouses for centuries after, as most houses didn’t have indoor plumbing. It wasn’t until the end of the 1800s — after inventor Alexander Cumming [improved](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=supe2r&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com) toilet design with the S-bend, which trapped smells — that toilets became common in homes, especially in upper-class households, and even then they were initially kept separate from the bathtub and sink, in a room referred to as [the “water closet](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=supe2r&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com).” Noting the lack of sanitation caused by pipes and traps running from room to room, health care professionals began urging architects to streamline their plumbing into a single location. Architects relented, and the “bathroom” was born.
---
By the late 1800s, most upper-class homes in the United States and the U.K. were outfitted with a tub, toilet, and sink, and middle-class homes followed soon after. In the wake of the First World War, bathrooms became increasingly common in working-class households, but still weren’t universal in the United States until the middle of the 20th century. Advances in plumbing led to the mass-production of the affordable, two-piece toilets still used today, and made the bathroom a household staple.
[](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=supe2r&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com) | [](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=supe2r&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)
---
By clicking a link in this email, you're agreeing to our [Terms of Use](https://track.historyfacts.com/?xtl=2kh5r9m0gvx4tc81eyau7lpp0u0je1ubr00tdamqt6wt6vorop6j8a6900fcathgyfijfax2xbv9si25cre6jg6w30khscudm95u89oqulan4b0m9ncygeopwak0g77ispdwvyhz65q6vvoo1t8ys7nbojwhid25ek8ui2dvfx650&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=supe2r&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com) and [Privacy Policy](https://track.historyfacts.com/?xtl=463ko1dghoveau9yyo7p03zd7h3vs4bmpm5hrjmwz6u1ncep16do5ecsff5q3mgar5n1opopfknb0n9ckoobvvqks1fs4kd2jdma1b7o2u8oecsp5vjqa6ijb6huqte52y6vatmpyoqlim2zny6zm68lxd6e7c3ua0i18fvpy6k0fuxx&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=supe2r&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com).
---

---|---|---
## Queen Victoria brought bathrooms to Buckingham Palace.
Since indoor plumbing was still in its infancy at the time, London’s Buckingham Palace lacked a three-piece bathroom when Queen Victoria arrived in 1837. In the late 19th century, a sanitary engineer named Thomas Crapper developed the refillable tank and opened the bathroom fittings showroom — a venue in which bathroom fittings were displayed and available for purchase. This boosted the market for indoor plumbing and helped make toilets acceptable to the upper class. Concealed plumbing also made flushing toilets more aesthetically pleasing, and as bathrooms grew in popularity, so did their number at Buckingham Palace. After adopting the Optimus — a toilet created in 1870 whose pipes were concealed by a large chair — Queen Victoria arranged for full bathrooms to be [installed](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=supe2r&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com) at the royal residence in the late 1880s. Today, the palace boasts 78 working bathrooms.

name: Team Domenici
subject: Statement from Nella Domenici Regarding 2026 Election
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Nella for Senate
body:
| | | | |
---
| **FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE****April 14, 2025**
**Santa Fe, N.M.** — Today, Nella Domenici released the following statement regarding the 2026 election:
**“I am deeply touched by the outpouring of support I received in last year's United States Senate race as well as the extraordinary encouragement from around the state to be your governor. While I am honored, I have determined that the best way to help the state that I love is to dedicate my efforts outside of government. Therefore, I have chosen not to run for governor or any other statewide position.**
**“Like my father, Senator Pete Domenici, and my family have done for decades, I will stay involved in and serve the New Mexico community. My focus will continue to be on healthcare, education, job creation, economic development, and public safety. Raising awareness for critical opportunities and issues and being a voice for those in need will remain a priority.**
**“My sincere appreciation extends to all of you who care passionately about improving New Mexico and for contributing your time and energy. I look forward to working along side you as I rejoin the private sector.”**
**###**

clean_body: | | | | | | FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASEApril 14, 2025 Santa Fe, N.M. — Today, Nella Domenici released the following statement regarding the 2026 election: “I am deeply touched by the outpouring of support I received in last year's United States Senate race as well as the extraordinary encouragement from around the state to be your governor. While I am honored, I have determined that the best way to help the state that I love is to dedicate my efforts outside of government. Therefore, I have chosen not to run for governor or any other statewide position. “Like my father, Senator Pete Domenici, and my family have done for decades, I will stay involved in and serve the New Mexico community. My focus will continue to be on healthcare, education, job creation, economic development, and public safety. Raising awareness for critical opportunities and issues and being a voice for those in need will remain a priority. “My sincere appreciation extends to all of you who care passionately about improving New Mexico and for contributing your time and energy. I look forward to working along side you as I rejoin the private sector.” | | | | | | | | | | | [Paid for by Nella for Senate.]() | mailto:info@nellaforsenate.com?subject=&body= Copyright (C) 2025 NELLA FOR SENATE All rights reserved. | | | | | | | | Nella for Senate PO Box 92918 Albuquerque, NM 87199-2918 * USA or |

# **$26 for 2026**
---
| [](https://click.nodemleftbehind.com/click/gpnv-2x48xj-w98o8d-kyd76rh8/)
---
| | | | Dear friend, The 2026 midterms are closer than they seem, and preparation starts now. This election could be our turning point to weaken Trump’s MAGA agenda and take bold steps toward real progress in this country. Trump may still occupy the White House until 2028, but in 2026, we can disrupt his plans, win back key seats, and stop the chaos he and his allies have unleashed. Every seat we flip brings us closer to restoring accountability, justice, and opportunity for everyone in America. That’s why we’re calling on our grassroots team—on you—to help us build a winning coalition. We’ve set an ambitious goal to fund voter outreach and organize in battleground districts across the country. [**_We’re asking for $26 for 2026—or whatever you can give—to fuel this effort. Every dollar counts as we work to protect democracy and push back against MAGA extremism. Let’s show MAGA that their days of chaos are numbered. Together, we can make 2026 a turning point for our country._**](https://click.nodemleftbehind.com/click/gpnv-2x48xj-w98o8e-kyd76rh9/) In Solidarity, The No Dem Left Behind Team

| | | About Blue Amp Action: We cannot sugarcoat the current situation. Right-wing authoritarianism, defeated by a worldwide coalition during WWII, was only in hibernation. Over the past decade it has infected democratic societies--including ours. It is as simple as it is terrifying: What we do now will define the future of our country and world. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal pols and pundits. **And savvy grassroots activists like you know, just like we do, that we can’t stop watching the people we help elect:_ after the elections, we have to keep organizing support for the policies we like and pushing back against the ones we don’t._** **__When the public tunes out between elections and the only people our elected officials hear from are the paid lobbyists of wealthy corporations, our government stops being accountable to the needs of ordinary families.__** **Democracy never sleeps, and neither can we.** So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in democracy, not dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do! — _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T9cfc1968-76de-49b2-b050-493d3832337f/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._

Texas A&M’s baseball team hit two grand slams in the ninth inning of Saturday’s home game against South Carolina. The 15–12 walk-off win marked the program’s [biggest comeback victory](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyckMHOnCAURp8Gd0wA0dEFiyaNu7_bLs0VLkr_i1jBcebtG32Ebu-5Oflyfn-NXxAorPMviGjK26fRpWMinHdYXSaIecVzSxQsjkqohgvNpR4dBPqMunJmEq6XFRr51LpVnW66ajEa-863nRK6cb1w2AvwU9OpZkL_lAKrYC6Z0FJLKUTTPGrRtZ230upW1L3umRboQkn7oyxY8A3Z0ye9cH_YFCsySylbZvUPpgamhvM8HzlciKnBJiKckamhQDyYGibIOAHRdblMHHjkFrbMk_cct2C5TREnsN_8lXlOR1m4hT1RWIGfoSz8BPq-v-8u_ArDhfyzb7r8jZ88s3o4ShxzOnaLrP5pFyjzVu6xu3HbGYhCbp9MizlCoBuU_wj-MupfAAAA__-soJJp) since at least 1974.

**Good Morning!** On this day in 1933, the [Panhandle-Plains Historical Museum](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyckL3OnDAQRZ_GdCDbDH-FizR0mzYlGvAAoww2wt5l9-2jzSN87b3SOdL585geyMJh-40Hufxe4-TjcxbaLgw-CR4p0H1G4YUmq21TaigNTB5ZPhMU3s3aD6YgZzqA1vbQ9MXukGaoO0MWWj2v1H6_bqnrfkBEXfcFuy9MgwFjtG6aqtZ926-LWaDV9QCDAk2ec7yqvFOmN6ZVPvFFV7XEoxC353wmVf9SdlR2vO-7ymnHGIQDVfHalB13DH6O8a-yI4V8MSVlxxPDdxcqT0EOqdw55XjxglIez0TPo7icP28W4dR2CvR2IMt_a_5BoJez_wIAAP__qiB2fQ) opened in Canyon, becoming the first state museum in Texas. It features a large collection of exhibits on the region’s anthropology, geology, and major industries. A Waco church enlisted the local sheriff's office for help with their annual Easter egg hunt. Check out [the story](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyc0LHOpCAUBeCnwQ4DeFUsKLah-7fd0iD3jpJBMcCMM2-_mXmEvzrFSb6cnH8_848LMRzrX7eTqa9bmjE9lkhrdgeW6PZy0HWmGDzNSqieC-ASZnQhvmdo0CwCJ9mQkSPAoDT0utmM73HsNIIbx2VZfA8atPO6n7pRjBKhCeaDCZAgpRB933ZCD_rmpYdBdBNMDARhqCm3daNKL1du8Z2elFuf9iaardazsO4PU5Ype11Xe7_q61MyZT82U1YAU1Yqpuz2ODATFn4PWPjq6kaZbxSDT2elzMmVb6wrx5xOntMSjpIOpmyTDZ5XiDGUYWQg1t2F-B1Rf_HX06j_AQAA__8lIHjj) in today's _Et Cetera_ section, and let us know what you think about Easter candy in our poll. Speak the language and skip the tourist traps with today's sponsor, [Babbel](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyckE1qwzAQRk8j72JG0sg_Cy0KxrsUShddGtkaxwL5B2sSJ7cvSXqCbmcej4_3c-7OLsSwXD7dTJbv49r59dpHuuxu8Sm6OS10bGsMA3UKlDkBniR23oX46DDztgdfy4ysLBELVaGpssmqCmr0boQBRteP1PvRSKyqsvQwOFJZsE8ZoEQpAYzJNVRFNQ5ywAJ0jbVAIB943XOeiOnu0hgf6432fFjnLNqJeUtCfwjVCtUex5HPFBamfaCNn4xQrS5rrb6Eao05F99PUrfX4IVuJJq6EKpI114K3fBEf_bEjun9UEI3Cx0pEjPt75sWugHsJL5aZLv12xFiDKkoBcJldiG-9vE_Ut6s-g0AAP__hht_GA) language learning software.

## Patrick Running for Fourth Term
Lt. Gov. Dan Patrick announced Friday he will [run for a fourth term](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyckLuOqzAUAL_GdCDbHBwoXNyGLrfdEvlxDNb6gWwnJH-_yn7C9qMZab7u21354NP-X0WU7eXyZvNDB9yLSrYGFWvC68zBG9w45VNPoWewWeXDe4POSk3twjqU7AYg-AzT3B2SgZiUBqcdmBH4uHC6aGGtdpPWi-Kdlx8ZBQaMUTpNw0hnMTvDDAg6LrAQoGh9y2VoBzZ8qerCOz-xDCbHLsijtbOS8R_hK-HrdV2Dyy8ujvyoLacPRPia8KqEr1al_lStePPdGxVP5ffUt_KIZ4_J5lIxYmpdkfa8fAi-ihsBukflw2-t_WHMU_KfAAAA__9ShXPY), touting an endorsement from President Donald Trump, who called him an “incredible friend.” Patrick served two terms in the Texas Senate before he was elected its presiding officer in 2014, [ousting former Lt. Gov. David Dewhurst](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyckLGypCAURL8GM6bgzh3FgGATsrfphhbCVamHYAGOb_5-y_2Ejbur-9T58zV92RBDWn_bnXT7WfLk8zlHWotNvka710TXkWNwNIGAFxfIJU7ehviZsPN6Fn6UHWk5IPag8KW6TbtxsYSoRlR-QWWX56xET0rM_aAGhC7oe0ygRCmFeL0eT6F6tTjpsBfPEUeGgnxouTzaRo1-bF3iJ7-pPFzeu6i31o7Knr8YGAbmuq6Hi8F9w5bP2nK6WwxMoqsyMDE7GxmY-5OBEcjASHkHja_5zb1N_LCtBPfNbUr5TI4qP6JNlbfMy5n4kgsHAT0vRJFcCzkxMF3R_rhCjKH2A0Ox7jbEf4TtP2S-NfwNAAD__zVIghQ) in the Republican primary. During his tenure, Patrick has advocated for state-funded school choice, property tax reform, election integrity, and border security. This session, he has focused on [banning THC-infused products](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyckDFuhDAQRU9jOpA9HgwULtLQbdqUyNjDMorByDjL7u2jzRFSf-m_p_d1m26OI-_3T7eRLc8lTSH9zJHu2e3hjG47d7qOFNnTBBLaWmKtcAqO42vCKthZhkFVZFWHaKDHtq9WOyyDlmo2zqNRrUaYVUewtGCWbnBzX7F9n0lUqJSUbdto2Zt-8cqjkXrAQaCkwCXlpqxU6OnOJb7Sg3Lj01ZFu5ZynEJ_CBgFjNd1NX7NaX-vAsa3cGF_ChhdLuwjCRiD2-vDlcz-u6ZIvnDaa5DQKa1Nc6xHlW04Lo6RT9MJlPfNcfzjlX-keVj4DQAA___PH3G9), which have proliferated in the state since a 2019 law legalized the sale of items containing less than 0.3% tetrahydrocannabinol (THC), the psychoactive ingredient in marijuana. Patrick has a 37% approval rating, while 32% of Texans disapprove, according to a [February 2025 poll](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyckL2OwyAQhJ8Gd7YWsv4rKK5xl2uvtDawJOiwQYDz8_an5BGuHGk08-n7Oa9n8sHv12_aWNeni6uNxyXwNdNuS6Ct7PxIMXjDqwLVt4CtxNWSD68VG6svYGfZsJYj4qAm7KfmptkZNw6jJKCZRwCWzgISUj_TyGwbr99jgBKlBOj77gTTMDkjDQ5wmnEWCGx9jbmrN678pOLCK945dyZuTdC3WlMRpy-hFqGWT-FNWb0p3fGJHdtDqKVwFWqxtLeJavbmt6WUcrxTaB1f8kH51b5ZmqxtevgQfBlGgXDdyIfPWf2Hl7tWfwEAAP__KNxzOQ) conducted by the University of Texas. Among Republicans, Patrick’s approval stands at 65%.
## Border Park Reopened to Public
Shelby Park in Eagle Pass has [reopened to the public](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyckD3O3CAQQE-DO1aAx38FRRp3mzalhWGwUTAgYP9uH7HKCb5mpNE8PT3Nn_t2V867cPxWF8r6tnEz8bF7PLIKpnh1lYCvFL3TuAkmBsqActiMcv6zQWfkzszCO5R8AhjFDMPcnZL1HLEfx4FDP41WWQ3ItJgmJZZl6OfOySZjwIFzxobh1rN5nK3mGkbWL7AQYGhcjflWT6z4VsX6T3xivul4dV6etaZC-l9ErESsJaGu-XH5qJUP-CqNImKt73aMj3rSr4Oip0mVSMTaKCLWlkHEyqCNiYg1qfyXZowJQ6HKVsx0RxcOislpDG2P9r-N7jEbzPRypbgYuixNejnvXRknAuy4lPPf3vqD1z6l-BcAAP__-xiIbA) after being locked down for over a year due to heightened border security measures. The 47-acre park had been occupied by the Texas Military Department since early 2024, sparking a [standoff with the Biden administration](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyckLGuozAQRb8GOke2GRwoXGyTLttuiQY8gLXGY9nekOzXPyV6X_Dao6ure8-f-3RHH3zcfuNBtj5Xnhz_mwNtGaMrAY8S6Uwc_EKTlroXEoSCyaEPrwlaZ2fpRtWSVVcAowfoh3a3qleDGYfO9N2qx1VCRwZ6wCuA1IC69fZdJkGBUlL2_aWTgxnWRS1gZDfC2IAk5yvnS92p0hPLGl78oHxZ-GiD3WtNpel-NfrW6Fuks0DBiLFy9PzOfNNG32r-PBJlZ66U36jsFOaXSJj_ikycKBbhoyDcAomEpYiZs6MsOFHG6jkKx2cs_j-5NluXTh-CL-bagNwO9OGzqv5A4MPqrwAAAP__TdqBoA), whose Border Patrol was ordered to remove razor wire fencing installed by Texas National Guard troops. Although the Supreme Court ruled in favor of the Biden administration, Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton [refused to grant federal agents access to the park](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyckL2urCAUhZ8GOwzg9q-guI3d3PaUZitbJYNggBnHtz9xHuF0K_myVr6sn8f4QOusX__jTjp_ljCa8JocrRG9SQ735Ok8grMzjUqomgvgEkaD1l0jFEZPwvSyIC1bgEZ1UHfFpqWZsO-MomrpJ5S9MALqtgbVKoDOLIXV95gACVIKUddlJbqmW2Y5QyOqHnoGgozNIZZ5o0wfTIu7wptiOYe9cHrL-Uis-sfUwNRwnmc5T7dqujlTwx2ZGr5NjjmH6OniK3mK6PiTPD_wk4PnkZZXosQXMl-EK_mcOM4zpcTTRm66-IHxyQlXR_zAlPgUoqHI1FBEbY7TOmdT0zIQ647WfRXzH958a_UbAAD__0BGhvY). In the year before the state’s seizure of Shelby Park, illegal border crossings averaged between 2,000 and 3,000 per day. By April of last year, that number had dropped to about five per day—and now [it's nearly zero](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyckL2OhCAUhZ8GO8wF8a-g2MZutt3SIFyV7FWIMOP49hvnEbY9J9_Jl_PzGB_Gk9-Xb7Ohzu85jC48J8LlMLtLZLa04xkDeYujBFlzUFyo0RlP16gKpydwvShQi1apRnaq7opVg6uhbZToWhT11FfgGtG1jXBVBcK1pvD6HgMllBAAdV1W0DXdbIVVDVS96pkCdD6Ho8wrZnybNNMVXniUNmwF6TXnmFj1xeTA5HCeZ2mnWzXdPZPDB2FyuCMmh7QiTReP5vjlaBZCHk1K_MAQcU88Pifyls-BKJx-X_gH55snn81x8WDtM5rdXkwOxaFdPD2RT03LFCyb8fSxyv848KXlXwAAAP__VvGBSA).
## Aggies Win on Double Grand Slams in Ninth
Texas A&M’s baseball team hit two grand slams in the ninth inning of Saturday’s home game against South Carolina. The 15–12 walk-off win marked the program’s [biggest comeback victory](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyckMHOnCAURp8Gd0wA0dEFiyaNu7_bLs0VLkr_i1jBcebtG32Ebu-5Oflyfn-NXxAorPMviGjK26fRpWMinHdYXSaIecVzSxQsjkqohgvNpR4dBPqMunJmEq6XFRr51LpVnW66ajEa-863nRK6cb1w2AvwU9OpZkL_lAKrYC6Z0FJLKUTTPGrRtZ230upW1L3umRboQkn7oyxY8A3Z0ye9cH_YFCsySylbZvUPpgamhvM8HzlciKnBJiKckamhQDyYGibIOAHRdblMHHjkFrbMk_cct2C5TREnsN_8lXlOR1m4hT1RWIGfoSz8BPq-v-8u_ArDhfyzb7r8jZ88s3o4ShxzOnaLrP5pFyjzVu6xu3HbGYhCbp9MizlCoBuU_wj-MupfAAAA__-soJJp) since at least 1974. The Aggies took an early 2-0 lead but [fell behind 12-2 in the sixth inning](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyckD2OgzAQRk9jOiN7GAgULrZxl223RP4ZgoXBCHtDcvsVOcK2b6Sned_PfbybEMP2-DYrqfKa0ujTr430OMzmczRr3ujcUwyORhDQcoFc4uhNiO8RK6-s8IOsSMkbYgc9tn01q9aAbdrB4M2RGMTQd9ZOFyILkoyrgrpkAiVKKUTb1o3ou35y0mEnmgEHhoJ8KOmoy0yFXiZP8Z2edNQurVVUcyl7Zs0XA81An-dZL7a8riMDfbkZaIEMtGwY6IW2kvlp4sLTNPFPHL_quEvrHqlQ5nPIJR3BXYiscQsDXR3K72eIMeTuxlA8VhPi54Pyj7GeCv4CAAD___zsd-4). They had cut the lead to 12-7 going into the ninth, when Hayden Schott and Kaeden Kent hit grand slams to achieve the historic comeback. Gov. Dan Patrick announced Friday he will , touting an endorsement from President Donald Trump, who called him an “incredible friend.” Patrick served two terms in the Texas Senate before he was elected its presiding officer in 2014, in the Republican primary. During his tenure, Patrick has advocated for state-funded school choice, property tax reform, election integrity, and border security. This session, he has focused on , which have proliferated in the state since a 2019 law legalized the sale of items containing less than 0.3% tetrahydrocannabinol (THC), the psychoactive ingredient in marijuana. Patrick has a 37% approval rating, while 32% of Texans disapprove, according to a conducted by the University of Texas. Among Republicans, Patrick’s approval stands at 65%. Border Park Reopened to Public Shelby Park in Eagle Pass has after being locked down for over a year due to heightened border security measures. The 47-acre park had been occupied by the Texas Military Department since early 2024, sparking a , whose Border Patrol was ordered to remove razor wire fencing installed by Texas National Guard troops. Although the Supreme Court ruled in favor of the Biden administration, Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton . In the year before the state’s seizure of Shelby Park, illegal border crossings averaged between 2,000 and 3,000 per day. By April of last year, that number had dropped to about five per day—and now . Aggies Win on Double Grand Slams in Ninth Texas A&M’s baseball team hit two grand slams in the ninth inning of Saturday’s home game against South Carolina. The 15–12 walk-off win marked the program’s since at least 1974. The Aggies took an early 2-0 lead but . They had cut the lead to 12-7 going into the ninth, when Hayden Schott and Kaeden Kent hit grand slams to achieve the historic comeback. Only 24 teams have ever hit two grand slams in the same inning, the . The feat has happened just four times in the past 20 years.
