name: Bryan
subject: April 14 Campaign Update
body:
We had a major victory for election integrity last week!

| | | | | [](https://bryansteil.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=5855432ef30dec79be4122095&id=3fbe88867e&e=e907534017)
---
| | | | | **April 14, 2025**
---
| Here’s an update from our campaign:
---
| | | | |
# Securing our Elections
---
| Last week, the House voted to pass the [_**SAVE Act**_](https://bryansteil.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=5855432ef30dec79be4122095&id=0f0e716fcd&e=e907534017) _._ This strong election integrity measure ensures that _**only**_ U.S. citizens are voting in our elections by requiring proof of citizenship and strengthening voter ID standards.
---
[](https://bryansteil.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=5855432ef30dec79be4122095&id=74141fcee6&e=e907534017)
| I was proud to lead the fight to pass this bill joined by my colleague Chip Roy. This bill helps codify [**President Trump’s executive order**](https://bryansteil.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=5855432ef30dec79be4122095&id=33fdf9a4d3&e=e907534017) on election integrity from earlier this year.
Despite the fact that [**83%**](https://bryansteil.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=5855432ef30dec79be4122095&id=c9c84cd3dd&e=e907534017) of Americans believe that only U.S. citizens should be voting in our elections, [**208 Democrats**](https://bryansteil.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=5855432ef30dec79be4122095&id=0f580113ae&e=e907534017) chose to oppose this legislation.
This bill now heads to the Senate for a vote before it can be signed into law by President Trump.
---
[](https://bryansteil.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=5855432ef30dec79be4122095&id=fb9cd29bef&e=e907534017)
| While this is an important first step in securing our elections, we must build on this success with additional legislation and continue our work to root out potential fraud and vulnerabilities in fundraising platforms like [**ActBlue**](https://bryansteil.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=5855432ef30dec79be4122095&id=987e4e99cd&e=e907534017).
I’m committed to continuing my work, not just to secure our elections, but also to secure our border, to lower costs for Wisconsin families, and to reign in out-of-control government spending.
---
| **On Wisconsin,** Bryan Steil
---
| [DONATE](https://bryansteil.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=5855432ef30dec79be4122095&id=cf2602b589&e=e907534017)
---
|
---

clean_body: We had a major victory for election integrity last week! | | | | | | | | | | April 14, 2025 | Here’s an update from our campaign: | | | | | Securing our Elections | Last week, the House voted to pass the . This strong election integrity measure ensures that only U.S. citizens are voting in our elections by requiring proof of citizenship and strengthening voter ID standards. | I was proud to lead the fight to pass this bill joined by my colleague Chip Roy. This bill helps codify on election integrity from earlier this year. Despite the fact that of Americans believe that only U.S. citizens should be voting in our elections, chose to oppose this legislation. This bill now heads to the Senate for a vote before it can be signed into law by President Trump. | While this is an important first step in securing our elections, we must build on this success with additional legislation and continue our work to root out potential fraud and vulnerabilities in fundraising platforms like . I’m committed to continuing my work, not just to secure our elections, but also to secure our border, to lower costs for Wisconsin families, and to reign in out-of-control government spending. | On Wisconsin, Bryan Steil | | | | | | Steil for Wisconsin, Inc. PO Box 103 Oak Creek, WI 53154-8618 * USA or |

name: Ernest Lineberger
subject: Update from Ernest Lineberger, April 14, 2025
body:
Hi Friends of Lineberger for US Congress!

This is a quick update on some coming events.

We have had some very successful protest rallies to come together and show
that we are not happy with the direction the administration is taking our
government infrastructure and the impacts to our neighbors and community.
We had an estimated 900 people at the Flower Mound Parker Square protest on
April 5th. Sanger had a smaller but equally energetic group on April 13th.

[image: image.png]

There are more events coming as we work to grow our base, listen to our
community, and work to win enough hearts and minds to convert to a winning
vote total in 2026.

Tuesday, April 15th, 6:30 PM ‘Beyond the Ballot: Sustaining Our Strength
for the Next Fight’
Dallas College Coppell Center, 101 S. Royal Ln, Coppell, TX

Thursday, April 17th, 6:30 PM ‘Town Hall in Aubrey’ with Linsey Fagan and
Ernest Lineberger
Navo Middle School, 1701 Navo Rd, Aubrey, TX

Friday, April 18th, 11:30 AM, ‘South Wise Republican Lunch’ with Brandon
Gill
Big Tony’s BBQ Kitchen, 3527 TX-114, Rhome, TX

Saturday, April 19th, Block Walking for Local Democratic Candidates
Check Mobilize for one near you

Sunday, April 20th, Easter Blessings to All

Monday, April 21st, Passover Begins

Tuesday, April 22nd, Early voting begins and runs through April 29th

Tuesday, April 26th, Denton Town Hall with Beto O’Rourke
https://mobilize.us/s/e5kMne

Wednesday, April 30th, LULAC 4366 Scholarship Awards Banquet

Saturday, May 3rd, Election Day for Local and Municipal Elections!!!

Sunday, May 4th, 2 – 4 PM, ‘Rally at Denton Square’

Saturday, May 10th, 10 AM, ‘Kite Flying with Carrollton Pride’

Tuesday, May 13th, 6 – 8 PM, ‘Town Hall at Robson Ranch’ with Ernest
Lineberger

Saturday, May 17th, 6 – 10 PM, ‘Footloose in Denton County’
This is a critical fundraiser for Meals on Wheels Denton County and Span
Not Political, but they need our help especially with issues with other
funding.
Span and Meals on Wheels <https://span-transit.org/> Events -
Span and Meals on Wheels <https://span-transit.org/events/>

We are active with several campaign strategies in addition to the big
events above. I’m getting out to even more groups, meetings and events to
help share the message of my campaign. We are actively sending fundraising
emails. We are planning to launch some fundraising texting. Digital
Advertising is also coming soon to help spread our reach beyond those we
can reach with other methods.

Many of you have signed up on our website link to volunteer, and we have
our volunteer coordinator, Christian, reaching out to match volunteers with
current needs. Some have offered to host house party fundraisers, and we
have Briana, our house party coordinator/ scheduler, to help coordinate
getting events on the calendar and helping hosts with checklist and ideas
to make it successful.

Why are we up and running so strongly this early? Because there is a lot
of bad going on that we need to counter and give our community hope for a
better future. We hear it on the news. Government functions terminated.
Government employees fired. Student Visas cancelled. Budget threats to
our social safety net and health care services. It’s a lot. I’m eager to
hear from you on your particular circumstances and what is hitting you and
your neighbors as Project 2025 becomes more reality than we had hoped.
Tell me about it by using my free form survey: Share Your Concerns |
Lineberger for TX-26 <https://www.linebergerforuscongress.com/voice>

Thank you for all you are doing for our campaign and for your community.
Together we will weather the worst of this storm. By winning the election,
we can begin to right the ship, make the needed repairs for smoother
sailing to allow everyone to reach their full potential.

Putting People’s Needs First, Before Wealthy Greed, Before Party Politics!

Ernest Lineberger
Democratic Candidate for US House for CD26

name: Democrats.org
subject: Here’s what we have on record for you:
body:
---
Derek, If you’ve been waiting for the right moment to make your first contribution of 2025 to support the DNC, _this is it._ Here’s why: With Trump wreaking havoc on our economy and our democracy, it’s more important than ever that Democrats not only cut through the noise — but build the long-term infrastructure we’ll need to elect more leaders who can check Trump’s power in their states, cities, and districts. That’s exactly what we’re doing. So far this year, we’ve focused on organizing everywhere and investing in state parties. We’re seeing the results: Since the year began, Democrats have overperformed in 17 of 18 special elections, and won pivotal races in states like Wisconsin and Pennsylvania. On top of that, we’re working to meet voters where they are, day in and day out. We’ve recently launched the People’s Cabinet, a new project that will bring together experts and community leaders who will cut through the lies and speak directly to the American people about the reality of what the Trump administration is doing to our country. And we’ve launched a new War Room operation, focused on driving aggressive daily messaging to counter the Trump Administration, leveraging creative opposition tactics and an expansive research operation, and combating lies from the far-right. **To fund all of this work, we’re counting on your support. Here’s what we have on record for you so far this year:** _**Democratic National Committee 2025 Supporter Record**_ **Name:** Derek
**Supporter ID:** 22930333
**Email:** dpwillis67@gmail.com
**2025 Donor:** No — Not yet ❌
**Suggested Donation:[$3](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-april-2025?refcode=em_20250414_3189_fr&amount=3&refcode2=3189_22930333_3ACSuS&akid=3189%2E22930333%2E3ACSuS)**

name: Devan
subject: Will you join my new focus group? -->
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the DSCC | PO Box 96047, Washington, DC 20090 | **[dscc
body:
| | | Hi there,

I'm Devan, and I'm excited to be the DSCC's new executive director -- serving alongside Senator Schumer and our new Chair, Senator Kirsten Gillibrand, to take back the Senate majority.

Like starting any new job, the most important thing I can do _right now_ to lead this team to victory is to **listen.** And that means the most important thing YOU can do to help us is to join my new focus group of committed grassroots Democrats and share your insights from on the ground.

**[So please, will you join my focus group by taking this survey to help us lay the foundation and build a winning strategy? I'm ready to hear what Democrats think, so please get started as soon as you're able:](http://go.dscc.org/go/21470?ask=5&t=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-ND-20250414-Devan-NewEDFocusGroup-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-ND-20250414-Devan-NewEDFocusGroup-Pros-sl1&akid=20011%2E9716520%2E7bJKh0)**

| | [Join my focus group](http://go.dscc.org/go/21470?ask=5&t=6&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-ND-20250414-Devan-NewEDFocusGroup-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-ND-20250414-Devan-NewEDFocusGroup-Pros-sl1&akid=20011%2E9716520%2E7bJKh0)
---

I'm already hearing from the top experts in the Democratic Party, but I truly believe that's not enough -- and they'd tell you the same thing. I need to hear directly from grassroots supporters about what will define this election. **That's why I need _your_ help to shape the critical road ahead.**

I'm eagerly anticipating the first wave of responses to learn from your insights and gauge our party's motivation right out of the gate. I hope you won't wait any longer to respond.

**[Senator Gillibrand and I are ready to dig into your responses first thing tomorrow, so please, will you enter the focus group before midnight?](http://go.dscc.org/go/21470?ask=5&t=7&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-ND-20250414-Devan-NewEDFocusGroup-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-ND-20250414-Devan-NewEDFocusGroup-Pros-sl1&akid=20011%2E9716520%2E7bJKh0)**

| [Enter focus group](http://go.dscc.org/go/21470?ask=5&t=8&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-ND-20250414-Devan-NewEDFocusGroup-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-ND-20250414-Devan-NewEDFocusGroup-Pros-sl1&akid=20011%2E9716520%2E7bJKh0)
---

I'm looking forward to working with you,

Devan
DSCC Executive Director

* If you've recently responded or done so with a different email address, thank you! Online records may not be up to date, but don't worry -- your responses have been recorded.
| |

[](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/support-senate-democrats-2025-q2-recurring?refcode=DPE-footer&amount=5&recurring=1&t=1&refcode2=20011_9716520_7bJKh0&akid=20011%2E9716520%2E7bJKh0)
* * *
The DSCC is the only committee solely dedicated to supporting Democrats in the U.S. Senate, and our work relies on grassroots supporters like you. If you'd like to make a contribution to support us, please **[click here](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/support-senate-democrats-2025-q2?refcode=DPE-footer&t=2&refcode2=20011_9716520_7bJKh0&akid=20011%2E9716520%2E7bJKh0)**.

**[Click here](https://go.dscc.org/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=3&akid=20011%2E9716520%2E7bJKh0)** to receive fewer emails. **[Click here](https://go.dscc.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=4&akid=20011%2E9716520%2E7bJKh0)** to unsubscribe.

Paid for by the DSCC | PO Box 96047, Washington, DC 20090 | **[dscc.org](https://www.dscc.org/)** | Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.

name: Jackson Lahmeyer
subject: Dinner w/President Trump For Easter | God Is Moving In America!
body:
So much has changed in America... for the better! Last year, President Joe Biden declared Easter Sunday to also be Trans National Visibility Day whic
| | |
---
So much has changed in America... **for the better!** Last year, **President Joe Biden** declared **Easter Sunday** to also be **Trans National Visibility Day** which was a _slap in the face_ to every Christian in America. This year however, it is a very different story. Under **President Trump** there will be a _HUGE_ celebration this **Holy Week for Easter**. I am beyond excited to join a few other Pastors for an _Easter Dinner_ at the **White House** with **President Trump**. God is moving in our country! **Easter is about Christ once again in America!!** If you have not read the proclamation from **President Trump** about this Holy Week and Easter then you need to. **This is what I voted for!** Read it here: [Presidential Message on Holy Week, 2025](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=20695285.11337.1.da6d44af20a680c55cd3de6510110d50) God Bless America! Thank you, Pastor Jackson Lahmeyer
---
|
---
| | [ Click Here: Presidential Message on Holy Week, 2025 ](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=20695285.11338.1.967299a2857be28ad714d28349d5c6d0)
---
---
| [ ](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=20695285.11339.1.1d9b8e47176dda50f21a02529bdc03e7)
---
One thing _I can verify_ is that being in ministry is sometimes difficult. In fact, the **New York Times** produced a survey about Pastors that indicate somewhere around **1500 leave the ministry** each month in America and _"57% of Pastors would leave ministry if they thought there was somewhere else to go."_ Pastors and Ministry Leaders are always being a blessing to their congregations and communities but sometimes they themsleves are _under appreciated_. Therefore, on June 5 at [Sheridan.Church](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=20695285.11340.1.7d8dc8d9a42f5b816701b8fd8aebfba0) we will be holding **A NIGHT OF BLESSING** specifically for Pastors and Ministry Leaders. **Tim Tebow** will encourage, **Colton Dixon** will sing, the **food** will be good and **gifts** will be given away. This is a **PRIVATE + FREE** event for Pastors and Ministry Leaders. **However, I have 3 asks:** _First_ , **Share this with your Pastor**! Pastors and Ministry Leaders can RSVP here: [https://www.tickettailor.com/events/sheridanchurch/1665203](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=20695285.11341.1.85871eceb75f3537aa7a780395038b06) _Second_ , If you are a business owner **consider sponsoring the event**. Sponsors will help cover the costs to be a blessing to the Pastors that will be in attendance. To sponsor this event visit [BUSINESS SPONSOR](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=20695285.11342.1.d154802066708d3f4eb303c386aab738) or email me at **jlahmeyer@sheridan.church** _Third_ , **Pray for this night**. There might be a Pastor on the verge of calling it quits that just needs a nudge of encouragement to stay in the game. Thank you, Pastor Jackson Lahmeyer
---
| | [ RSVP: NIGHT OF BLESSING ](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=20695285.11343.1.37df80741a4640c9a40ae0bfef0222a1)

---
---

name: Bonta HQ
subject: This isn’t just cruel. It’s unconstitutional.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ROB BONTA FOR CALIFORNIA ATTORNEY GENERAL 2026 FPPC ID #145642
body:
Trump’s message: Fall in line or face deportation.
# [](https://act.robbonta.com/go/3?refcode=em20250414&t=1&akid=1735%2E1088799%2EcNMXP1)
Donald Trump is sending a stunning message to campuses across the nation: **fall in line or face deportation.**
Without notice or clear reason, Trump is targeting international students and faculty for exercising their right to free speech. Hundreds have already had their visas revoked. Some have even been detained and arrested—without clear justification—just for expressing views Trump doesn’t like.
It’s unconstitutional.
That’s why last week, **Attorney General Bonta joined a coalition of 19 other state attorneys general to submit an amicus brief and challenge this so-called “Ideological Deportation Policy,”** which relies on a set of executive orders directing federal agencies to investigate, detain, and deport noncitizens based on their political speech—not actual threats to public safety.
In California alone, nearly 100 students across UC and CSU campuses, and Stanford have had their visas revoked. These are students who contribute to our economy, enrich our communities, and bring invaluable perspectives to our academic institutions.
AG Bonta is fighting to put a swift stop to Trump’s student visa revocation policy before it does any more damage.
**[Will you chip in today to support Rob Bonta and help him continue defending our constitutional rights from Trump’s attacks?](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rb-email-20250414?refcode=em20250414-1735&amount=5&refcode2=1735_1088799_cNMXP1&akid=1735%2E1088799%2EcNMXP1)**
**[Donate $5 »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rb-email-20250414?refcode=em20250414-1735&amount=5&refcode2=1735_1088799_cNMXP1&akid=1735%2E1088799%2EcNMXP1)**
As California’s Attorney General, Rob will always stand up for immigrants, safeguard democratic norms, and defend the rule of law. This moment demands that kind of courage.
Let’s stand with Rob as he stands up for all of us.
—Team Bonta
* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rb-email-wrapper?refcode=em20250414&t=3&refcode2=1735_1088799_cNMXP1&akid=1735%2E1088799%2EcNMXP1)**

* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.robbonta.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=4&akid=1735%2E1088799%2EcNMXP1)

body:
“I have my own opinions and I have a right to express those opinions, even at school. This right is called free speech.”
Liam's school trampled his right to free speech.
| | | | _Please see a special message from our sponsor. If you would like to unsubscribe, visit this[link](http://links.redconservatives.com/s/u/OP_qWJfDhujOB-oM0yDWsCiDMAAB1--G2iMHVPHMmGxvqc0kFDCAbgPun7t3Lhj1Cc7h2oU9knPAZZQ0pB6lJdWSurr3K4SAPkRCJGVCMiPUh5em8B31CuDYMTQLGh33Pz8xiHvVkwFXj8zR2eHblLHRPMY4yyDuTZTFHXCQt_H3YXuHxx8WsFKn/qrWk6QonCZnxvGK-JmK_S4Vr3UUQOOiA/8)_

**[](http://links.redconservatives.com/s/c/0Imznh7JUq1hLyE5e41hS0ulzV6C04sEoIb4tAgdmUHmFt4EytQdZk_K9ScoClep0WQTYKWA98c7pVlUeiz3M12n68si8ZBPPpk60SeOXCEIkCXfsvG0WhlfOFa3Re9JBCKqyOFiXLBf-Kr7IHVqz6pDsy3c4v2jgqZP8xw1raVuzvjvGE4xyCls_RXXGdn4x9tsa7_gA0hRfzhTn-9sAQUazfe_M97Ivb79uqoVe8kiiPC7KjYE5tNKT85oEn1LYybNqwq7a3OkEUoHw-qNI8dN6zR9ErmXSkCaNBguOf5jsbIOkFd5u93sJpq5kiMYW238Fu8Y0DDlZ9-QuVhRwMCkDdtEpJUGVsfCkdqZJ2Zi1otE2K9ERvKXwAAUIrX_Nxsz1XlrPQvM-gpdgzpvQlyy7a0Dlk02ki_peadwIoz8fp7ua7Ou5BdP9wGBCplO/ytVJkmPQozKRtd4S4zoSWU2ShqVRHlPG/8)**
## **[Stand with Student Forbidden by School to Wear'Only Two Genders' Shirt](http://links.redconservatives.com/s/c/Q_j4YagJpCjqcXdVcdrHB6DliAEHTp2MW1fyoFqcG0l6H1KY0niaZJCgXWMtvl8v_Jx0l1caq1r7jLIlttLVjzSq-IIMqz-mK3q3MCdn6HvHO2WB46qv5l_VDeOU8uosnnR1o1Jdo7TRyHZ1ePi_LfJU-sAxciQgQcC9L1S0SvL1pZAr0sJ7BYkue-qajYdlTmTTA806kkZiaWP3x-ebEXRlkSudSmB11BoAWTYmn4Zk1nFkzYdE2Gp7W-8zYz1F2RoBFfIjJjpJpdUhW1wgtzdmAGiwO-0b7EJECD8S4Bnqp3EG7wJe5NaTTjcWj6UVQhgmGbqYwl0xg2_3BavXECWBliolAeg4pllR9sDdii_hI4Ii-NL-hpsIKIJRAgciaSKerS5jBgnT93lYMgB1KRt_BxD4mAVZF-RFP56NQnz6l1HnhGmNwmvWx5nAGVtV1w/kls_BAv4DLhxtzVFYHqoVjDXocbDnpVX/8)**
Dear Fellow American, "I have my own opinions and I have a right to express those opinions, even at school. This right is called free speech."

Liam Morrison spoke these words after school officials punished him for wearing a shirt stating, "There are only two genders."

At the time he first wore the shirt, Liam was a 12-year-old student. Liam didn't even make it past the first period before the school principal and counselor pulled him out of class and ordered him to remove his shirt or go home.

Liam politely declined and was sent home.

Public school officials cannot censor a 7th grader's speech by forcing him to remove a shirt that respectfully states scientific fact. This is a gross violation of the First Amendment.

Please stand with this brave young student as he challenges his school's violation of free speech. Show Liam he isn't the only one standing up for these truths.

[ SHOW YOUR SUPPORT](http://links.redconservatives.com/s/c/8E8Yxc9UFUvtzlcdFNJXjJH6MiFBBg9VorRsMaKzHk1uBm7GKfwD5p-hS9gR90dW6q-fJ5ogbUBWOWZ8_oL653O9PXGvXLG5UMocNDUM4gYO564frsOdqst1xDyPp2P71RiNBIR5-icn7Vrd2m_94yWNknPGcMcZOCQZt3Z0CTQsClDKUePYRxQs7D9VH-CdonCQRO3n_YrHHzvfebeEMlcS9v8KVygKd-0Yq8ONHTU8PgrkyEHsSaTy1AbWgtVpFS5M7wdTpH2bu-97idPa6lY_38E58yiNKx4gIQ0LZDkFJo6JcaubrhEiLf7uPdkslEw-Fh0t4VDmxX8OYp_RNfAU9GozYcs19iG89eG-NrLeQouJx1SyiQAq4mRoGWyy3dpCPl8wOXEVIC9l7Zd5SegZwXSWeeAujVItTPYenPaiBYTnD6Lj2fkeL5T5xRN3WA/iG6t33PP1rUZQ8FgOMJRS2MlKudtvvHM/8)
## **This isn 't just about a shirt**
The First Amendment protects people of all beliefs, especially people who disagree with the government. If you only have freedom to agree with those in power, you don't have freedom at all.

Alliance Defending Freedom and the Massachusetts Family Institute sued the Town of Middleborough and school officials in federal court on Liam's behalf.

This case isn't just about a shirt.
* It's about a public school telling Liam--and thereby all other students--he can't wear a shirt with a message that's true unless it's the same opinion as the government's.
* It's about a public school trying to force a student to forfeit his free speech when he walks into the school building.
* It's about a public school imposing its view on students--and censoring students who disagree.

After a federal court of appeals in Massachusetts ruled against Liam, we've petitioned the U.S. Supreme Court to hear his case.

Across the nation, government officials are trying to silence views that differ from radical gender ideology. And it's falling to brave young students like Liam to stand in opposition and not affirm lies when school officials, who should be modeling adulthood, have failed students.

Will you stand with Liam today?

[ SHOW YOUR SUPPORT ](http://links.redconservatives.com/s/c/guZQb6gyHOda72iZeZKDkpdlR70Dkp6G7b6cXI1nC2K667x4Bue43CoqqjGnt2sGiU3k8j621EG7lz_A3xzTOLo4X8_6a6SW9nC7D6aNsYOkwIcLfYvk6hy8zOOX6gx0aPtQpUCDVxCTkKGt_pXc2rvdum2x7va5OUo1d-0FRkeVbaXSkmQtbUUm5BaEuaI2cxf52GJXGK0taDTyATiwKgjmt3OC4XhaK0cevwF8GmhUgN3KYg7hY_TVfZ5qmPlcFJGU7R3zaOcnmTjm18rM3KGCGpXngK427sOwmiTmzvLchxq8S63SIzGN6Gxi5U6zMLKd389rfLmQ8l89IYHsF0A3WSbNZnjvxFJbov6qQ0Q5UzOVX6C6NeMuyjA_nKsdQ69i5lvzfiAK1h284FWIk4xJBsbeIDHHWiXc23uc3cvOir1BtP1jpz1rdCz_WoY2hQ/tRjQ1hiPTDZVaLjgt3Ijlc-RYY-GFx0n/8)

--Alliance Defending Freedom

P.S. Liam isn't the first student to be censored--and he won't be the last. clean_body: “I have my own opinions and I have a right to express those opinions, even at school. This right is called free speech.” Liam's school trampled his right to free speech. | | | | Please see a special message from our sponsor. If you would like to unsubscribe, visit this Dear Fellow American, "I have my own opinions and I have a right to express those opinions, even at school. This right is called free speech." Liam Morrison spoke these words after school officials punished him for wearing a shirt stating, "There are only two genders." At the time he first wore the shirt, Liam was a 12-year-old student. Liam didn't even make it past the first period before the school principal and counselor pulled him out of class and ordered him to remove his shirt or go home. Liam politely declined and was sent home. Public school officials cannot censor a 7th grader's speech by forcing him to remove a shirt that respectfully states scientific fact. This is a gross violation of the First Amendment. Please stand with this brave young student as he challenges his school's violation of free speech. Show Liam he isn't the only one standing up for these truths. This isn 't just about a shirt The First Amendment protects people of all beliefs, especially people who disagree with the government. If you only have freedom to agree with those in power, you don't have freedom at all. Alliance Defending Freedom and the Massachusetts Family Institute sued the Town of Middleborough and school officials in federal court on Liam's behalf. This case isn't just about a shirt. It's about a public school telling Liam--and thereby all other students--he can't wear a shirt with a message that's true unless it's the same opinion as the government's. It's about a public school trying to force a student to forfeit his free speech when he walks into the school building. * It's about a public school imposing its view on students--and censoring students who disagree. After a federal court of appeals in Massachusetts ruled against Liam, we've petitioned the U.S. Supreme Court to hear his case. Across the nation, government officials are trying to silence views that differ from radical gender ideology. And it's falling to brave young students like Liam to stand in opposition and not affirm lies when school officials, who should be modeling adulthood, have failed students. Will you stand with Liam today? --Alliance Defending Freedom P.S. Liam isn't the first student to be censored--and he won't be the last. Alliance Defending Freedom 15100 N. 90th Street, Scottsdale, Arizona 85260 .

name: Kirsten Gillibrand
subject: re: The largest Medicaid cut in American history.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Gillibrand for Senate
body:
It’s Kirsten Gillibrand. I’m reaching out once again because last week, the GOP ramped up their attacks on Medicaid.

Here’s what you need to know: House Republicans voted to move forward with the LARGEST Medicaid cut in American history.

This budget could become a reality if we don’t speak out and fight back.

Make no mistake: Donald Trump and Elon Musk are attempting to line the pockets of the ultra-wealthy, while allowing working-class Americans to suffer.

[**_Will you sign your name on my urgent petition to tell the GOP that folks across the country OPPOSE their plans to cut Medicaid?_**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/105836004/539547350/-1225918853?refcode=LOC_EM_PET_2025.04.14_FWD-Medicaid_B2_F1_S1_C1_NO_RS-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTkvMS83MjcyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyZGQ0MmNjMC04NDE5LWYwMTEtOGIzZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ks31bDAnh6LyS1m7iHln_7FXycboSaRCQAeEI2mnUwk=&emci=74e3b5b3-3a19-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&emdi=2dd42cc0-8419-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&ceid=7659029) Thank you for caring,

Kirsten Gillibrand — BEGIN FORWARDED MESSAGE — [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/105836005/539547351/-1225918853?refcode=LOC_EM_PET_2025.04.14_FWD-Medicaid_B2_F1_S1_C1_NO_RS-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTkvMS83MjcyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyZGQ0MmNjMC04NDE5LWYwMTEtOGIzZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ks31bDAnh6LyS1m7iHln_7FXycboSaRCQAeEI2mnUwk=&emci=74e3b5b3-3a19-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&emdi=2dd42cc0-8419-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&ceid=7659029) Kirsten here.

Let’s talk about the GOP’s latest antics.

The GOP Senate Majority Leader is rushing a vote on Donald Trump and Elon Musk’s massive spending cuts.

Republicans will say anything to justify lining the pockets of billionaires, so I want to lay out exactly what this vote means: | **(1)** Cutting Medicaid and SNAP benefits, leaving families with higher medical and grocery bills.

**(2)** Enormous tax cuts for the ultra-wealthy.

**(3)** Disaster for our economy and for working families.

[**Will you be one of the 50,000 signatures we need to tell Elon Musk, Donald Trump and the entire GOP to keep their hands OFF our Medicaid?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/105836006/539547352/-1225918853?refcode=LOC_EM_PET_2025.04.14_FWD-Medicaid_B2_F1_S1_C1_NO_RS-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTkvMS83MjcyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyZGQ0MmNjMC04NDE5LWYwMTEtOGIzZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ks31bDAnh6LyS1m7iHln_7FXycboSaRCQAeEI2mnUwk=&emci=74e3b5b3-3a19-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&emdi=2dd42cc0-8419-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&ceid=7659029) | | | [SIGN THE PETITION >>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/105836007/539547353/-1225918853?refcode=LOC_EM_PET_2025.04.14_FWD-Medicaid_B2_F1_S1_C1_NO_RS-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTkvMS83MjcyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyZGQ0MmNjMC04NDE5LWYwMTEtOGIzZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ks31bDAnh6LyS1m7iHln_7FXycboSaRCQAeEI2mnUwk=&emci=74e3b5b3-3a19-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&emdi=2dd42cc0-8419-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&ceid=7659029)

Thank you,

Kirsten

name: Jasmine Crockett
subject: From the bottom of my heart, y’all: THANK YOU!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Serve America PAC
body:
|
---
Folks, it’s Jasmine. I know you’ve heard from me and my team a lot over the past few days and weeks, but I just wanted to personally reach out to thank you for all your support. Right now, I’m getting attacked on all sides from Trump, Musk, and their MAGA cronies here in Congress. They’re trying to scare me into silence and crush my voice as they orchestrate an authoritarian takeover of the federal government. **But my pledge to you and all the American people is that I will never stop fighting back and speaking up.** I couldn’t pull it off without your support, and I’m so grateful to have tenacious grassroots champions like you on my team each and every day. [**And with so much on the line right now, I’m going to continue needing your support for the weeks and months ahead. If you’re able, I’d be grateful for your continued support — $5, $10, or more at a time, split with Serve America — to keep up the fight and hold MAGA Republicans accountable. name: Xavier Becerra
subject: For the California we all love
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Fight for Progress PAC and not authorized by any candidate or any candidate's committee
body:
I’ve spent my whole career taking on Donald Trump and the GOP – and winning. | [](https://fight4progress.org/becerra?t=5DWj5z2OrSTU&refcode=BLU_EM_FFP_2025-04-14_1&amount=25)
---
Hi, Peter, I’m Xavier Becerra and I’m so excited to share with you that I’m running to be California’s next governor. I hope you’ll give me a moment to share why.

I’ve spent my whole career taking on Donald Trump and the GOP – and winning.

When Trump attacked Californians, I sued him 122 times as Attorney General – and won. When Republicans tried to gut the Affordable Care Act, I took them to the Supreme Court – and won. When Big Pharma was ripping off Americans, I negotiated lower drug prices – and won.

Now, I’m running for governor because I know what it takes to win tough fights – and deliver for working families.

[_**If you want a fighter who will stand up to Trump and the GOP and get California back on track, I hope you’ll split a donation today between my campaign and Fight For Progress to help my campaign get off to a strong start. Every dollar we raise today will help us build the grassroots movement we need to win.**_](https://fight4progress.org/becerra?t=1YCmuTglrAb7&refcode=BLU_EM_FFP_2025-04-14_1&amount=25)
| [ DONATE ](https://fight4progress.org/becerra?t=R9kqqtDFJDwL&refcode=BLU_EM_FFP_2025-04-14_1&amount=25)
---
My family came here with $12 to pursue the California Dream. I was born and raised by my parents in Sacramento, where they taught my siblings and me the value of hard work.

Despite those humble beginnings, they were able to own a home, send the four of us kids to college or the military, and set aside enough to retire with dignity.

**To me, that’s the California Dream – and that’s what I’m fighting to protect.**

Because today, despite having the largest economy in the country, that dream of owning a home, raising a family, and retiring with dignity is all too hard for Californians to attain. That’s why I’m promising to build our state’s economy, create opportunities and lower costs for working families, and protect the California we all love.

Peter, I’ve spent most of my life serving the people of California in the state legislature, Congress, as Attorney General, and then as the Secretary of Health and Human Services.

After a lot of consideration and thoughtful conversations with my wife, Carolina, and our three daughters, one thing became clear: **I am not done serving our state.**

**It’s time for California to rise and build. I’m ready to lead our state at this critical moment, and I’m humbly asking you to join me. My team and I set a grassroots fundraising goal to raise $100,000 in honor of our launch, and any amount you can spare would mean so much.**

[_**Please split a donation of $25 – or anything at all – to my campaign and Fight For Progress now to invest in my campaign for governor and our shared future as Californians.**_](https://fight4progress.org/becerra?t=Z15iRqJn43Dl&refcode=BLU_EM_FFP_2025-04-14_1&amount=25)

body:

# [Housing in California is too expensive. Here's how we can fix it.](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2052568&post_id=161344853&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTYxMzQ0ODUzLCJpYXQiOjE3NDQ2NzMzNDIsImV4cCI6MTc0NzI2NTM0MiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTIwNTI1NjgiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.6AW4KWFnTq3-WrRjO-Mybv0_-d88pszhId5pIBK7Yrc)
### Our state's housing shortage is decades in the making. It's not going to be enough to build more housing; we've got to accelerate the pace of construction to get out of this mess.
| | [Katie Porter](https://substack.com/@katieporter)
---
| Apr 14
---
| [](https://substack.com/@katieporter)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2052568&post_id=161344853&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTYxMzQ0ODUzLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc0NDY3MzM0MiwiZXhwIjoxNzQ3MjY1MzQyLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMjA1MjU2OCIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.H6OQ8j5lx_b4lJPbFcsPFNGEedy3GKnHEm1l2rbw3As&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2052568&post_id=161344853&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTYxMzQ0ODUzLCJpYXQiOjE3NDQ2NzMzNDIsImV4cCI6MTc0NzI2NTM0MiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTIwNTI1NjgiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.6AW4KWFnTq3-WrRjO-Mybv0_-d88pszhId5pIBK7Yrc&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2052568&post_id=161344853&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTYxMzQ0ODUzLCJpYXQiOjE3NDQ2NzMzNDIsImV4cCI6MTc0NzI2NTM0MiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTIwNTI1NjgiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.6AW4KWFnTq3-WrRjO-Mybv0_-d88pszhId5pIBK7Yrc)
---
| | [](https://substack..6ce8FyC3w2UP-OppzXLNbz-0thf1nBfg9-yiw9iQbgg?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/katieporteroc/p/housing-in-california-is-too-expensive?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

The cost of housing should concern every Californian. It's making it hard for our state to compete for jobs, pushing some of our college grads to leave our state, and slowing our economic growth.
The facts are grim for both renters and homeowners.**But keep reading because there are solutions!**
More than half of California renters are "cost burdened," meaning they have to spend more than 30% of their income on rent. Nearly a third of renters are "severely cost burdened"--spending _more than 50%_ of their income on rent. At that point, renters cannot afford to pay for other essentials like medicine or childcare, and cannot even think about saving.
High rental costs make it nearly impossible for Californians to save for homeownership--especially given the [average](https://substack.com/redirect/7952088b-7e2b-422c-ade6-e37ade680d76?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) California home costs more than $780,000. This isn't just a coastal or urban problem; the average price of a home in Bakersfield or Fresno is about $400,000, and Stockton tops half a million.
For decades, our state's housing supply just hasn't kept pace with our population. A new [study](https://substack.com/redirect/a2305eaf-55fa-4413-912e-2fff7687a960?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) helps shed light on why.
And it shows that our housing cost crisis _can_ be fixed, if we elect a leader who is laser-focused on the problem and willing to tackle bad policies.
Compared to Texas, it is 2.5x more expensive in California to build market-rate rental housing. For affordable rentals, it's nearly 3x more costly. Part of the reason, the study's authors explain, is that it takes on average 22 months longer to bring a project to completion in California than in Texas. Why does time matter? Builders face rising costs for materials during that near two-year period, driving up the final cost. Permitting delays create uncertainty and drive up legal and compliance costs. Developers commit money for years before they can start getting a return on their investment when the project is complete, and renters ultimately pay those costs.
**The only way out of our housing crisis is to build a whole lot more housing.** Estimates put the exact number of homes that we need at somewhere between 1 million and 4 million.
**We 're only going to get there if we make construction cheaper and faster**. I have some ideas for how we get there, including greenlighting innovative building strategies and streamlining the permitting process. We need to level the "building" field, so to speak, with our competitor states. We don't have to sacrifice environmental or safety standards. What we need is certainty for people who build housing--certainty that their projects won't get mired in years of pointless delay. And certainty that government will invest in the infrastructure needed--roads, sewers, schools--to support new communities.
Housing is the top expense making life unaffordable for California families, so it needs to be the number one priority for California's next governor in helping Californians make ends meet.
And by the way, based on a [New York Times analysis](https://substack.com/redirect/8f899642-715d-4a8e-ba9f-c3aed9ef2e5e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), **Trump 's tariffs are predicted to drive up the cost of every single material in a home, **with estimates that roofing will cost 21% more, appliances will cost 11% more, and cabinets will cost 17% more, among other things. This is yet another example of how California's next leader needs to see how our state's challenges and Trump's actions are wedded together.
Our state's housing shortage is decades in the making. It's not going to be enough to build more housing; we've got to accelerate the pace of construction to get out of this mess and protect California's future. I'm willing to work with anyone, or take ideas from anywhere, to make our housing more affordable.
Katie Porter
**Paid for by Katie Porter for Governor 2026 FFPC #1479597**

clean_body: Our state's housing shortage is decades in the making. It's not going to be enough to build more housing; we've got to accelerate the pace of construction to get out of this mess. | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more Our state's housing shortage is decades in the making. It's not going to be enough to build more housing; we've got to accelerate the pace of construction to get out of this mess. | | | Apr 14 | | | | | | | | | | | | The cost of housing should concern every Californian. It's making it hard for our state to compete for jobs, pushing some of our college grads to leave our state, and slowing our economic growth. The facts are grim for both renters and homeowners.But keep reading because there are solutions! More than half of California renters are "cost burdened," meaning they have to spend more than 30% of their income on rent. Nearly a third of renters are "severely cost burdened"--spending more than 50% of their income on rent. At that point, renters cannot afford to pay for other essentials like medicine or childcare, and cannot even think about saving. High rental costs make it nearly impossible for Californians to save for homeownership--especially given the California home costs more than $780,000. This isn't just a coastal or urban problem; the average price of a home in Bakersfield or Fresno is about $400,000, and Stockton tops half a million. For decades, our state's housing supply just hasn't kept pace with our population. A new helps shed light on why. And it shows that our housing cost crisis can be fixed, if we elect a leader who is laser-focused on the problem and willing to tackle bad policies. Compared to Texas, it is 2.5x more expensive in California to build market-rate rental housing. For affordable rentals, it's nearly 3x more costly. Part of the reason, the study's authors explain, is that it takes on average 22 months longer to bring a project to completion in California than in Texas. Why does time matter? Builders face rising costs for materials during that near two-year period, driving up the final cost. Permitting delays create uncertainty and drive up legal and compliance costs. Developers commit money for years before they can start getting a return on their investment when the project is complete, and renters ultimately pay those costs. The only way out of our housing crisis is to build a whole lot more housing. Estimates put the exact number of homes that we need at somewhere between 1 million and 4 million. We 're only going to get there if we make construction cheaper and faster. I have some ideas for how we get there, including greenlighting innovative building strategies and streamlining the permitting process. We need to level the "building" field, so to speak, with our competitor states. We don't have to sacrifice environmental or safety standards. What we need is certainty for people who build housing--certainty that their projects won't get mired in years of pointless delay. And certainty that government will invest in the infrastructure needed--roads, sewers, schools--to support new communities. Housing is the top expense making life unaffordable for California families, so it needs to be the number one priority for California's next governor in helping Californians make ends meet. And by the way, based on a , Trump 's tariffs are predicted to drive up the cost of every single material in a home, with estimates that roofing will cost 21% more, appliances will cost 11% more, and cabinets will cost 17% more, among other things. This is yet another example of how California's next leader needs to see how our state's challenges and Trump's actions are wedded together. Our state's housing shortage is decades in the making. It's not going to be enough to build more housing; we've got to accelerate the pace of construction to get out of this mess and protect California's future. I'm willing to work with anyone, or take ideas from anywhere, to make our housing more affordable. Katie Porter Paid for by Katie Porter for Governor 2026 FFPC #1479597 | | | | | | | (C) 2025 Katie Porter Katie Porter for Governor 2026, PO Box 5176 Irvine, CA 92616, Email us: info@katieporter.com ? ?

body:
When we think about the people who have done the most to support VoteVets over the years, it’s not the people who write the biggest checks who come to mind.
#
Peter -
When we think about the people who have done the most to support VoteVets over the years, it’s not the people who write the biggest checks who come to mind.
It’s the people who were there when everything was on the line.
Like you, Peter.

Your support, voice, and participation have helped us run some of the most powerful and impactful ads out there. Your support helps us win races and pass legislation that improves lives.
Thanks to you we’ve won races that wouldn’t even be competitive.
It’s helped us fight for things that matter most to Veterans. We’ve killed repeated efforts to privatize the VA, to cut or shortchange care. We passed the PACT Act and expanded care to millions. It got hundreds of Veterans and National Security professionals elected last year, and they are the ones holding the line against Trump now.

Today, your donations are supporting our efforts to make Republicans in Congress and around the country pay a price for everything they allow Trump and Elon to do — from firing Veterans to imperling the VA.
But here’s an interesting fact: out of almost 2 million people who have taken action with VoteVets, only 1% have made a online donation to support our work.
Today, we’re asking you to join that select group.

Our average donation is $25. Some give more, many give less. But with the VA under attack like never before in its history, with Veterans being fired across the government, and with our commitment to our allies in question, all that matters is that you are chipping in to an organization that is leading the fight against Donald Trump every single day.
We’ll put it right to work fighting and defeating these liars and hypocrites in the most important moment in modern American political history — the one we’re in right now
[**If helping VoteVets run some of the most effective ads in politics, or taking the fight to Trump in Washington, or supporting our lawsuits against Elon Musk’s overreach seems worth $5 to you, we’re humbly asking you to make a donation. We really need you. Thank you.**](https://act.votevets.org/go/2927?c0=vv-pac-em-april25.G9aCQU&refcode=em250414-1-120&t=1&akid=34347%2E3013755%2EL39uyt)

clean_body: | | Hi there — On Thursday, I joined other members of Congress for a CNN town hall to answer the questions so many of our constituents are asking. As the only woman onstage, my Republican colleagues tried to talk over me, interrupt me, and mansplain my answers — but I was not having any part of that. I pushed back on their baseless lies and stood up for public education, seniors, our veterans, and the immigrant families who call our communities home. = As a mom and former national teacher of the year, I know that the best leaders are empathetic and compassionate. Right now, Donald Trump and Elon Musk are advancing policies that are exceptionally cruel. I did not hesitate to call out the misinformation and deflection tactics we have come to expect from so many Republicans. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your split donation to Jahana Hayes and Elect Democratic Women will go through immediately: | | = | = | = | = | = | = Thank you for your support, Jahana Hayes | | About Jahana** Jahana Hayes proudly represents Connecticut's 5th Congressional district. subject: Bernie Sanders is LEADING the fight >>
disclaimer_text: Paid for by VPP
body:
You need to see this:
| **🚨 BREAKING: DEMOCRATS FIGHT BACK! 🚨** [](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/89042?t=6&akid=19531%2E878179%2E8HUZ1b) Bernie Sanders is LEADING the charge to take on Trump and save our Democracy. We’re _THRILLED!_

**Trump and MAGA Republicans are already attacking him relentlessly, so we’re sending Sen. Bernie Sanders a MASSIVE letter thanking him for all his amazing work!**

[**You’ve been PERSONALLY selected to sign, friend, so please don’t let us down: Add your name to thank Senator Sanders now! »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/89042?t=7&akid=19531%2E878179%2E8HUZ1b) | [**Donate $3 to sign the card now »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/89042?t=8&akid=19531%2E878179%2E8HUZ1b)
---
TO: Sen. Bernie Sanders
FROM: A proud Democrat
Subject: Thank you. **Dear Senator Bernie Sanders,**

__Thank you for all your hard work leading the Anti-Trump resistance!__

It’s easy to feel helpless with MAGA Republicans in charge of the House, Senate, and White House, but you keep us hopeful that we can fight back.

**I know it isn’t easy fighting off Trump and the GOP’s escalating threats to take you down, so please know that Democrats _across the country_ stand with you.**

Your leadership is absolutely critical right now, and we hope more Democrats follow your lead.

[**I can’t thank you enough for all of your work to stop Donald Trump and save our Democracy! So I’m signing this letter and chipping in $3 to the Voter Protection Project to show my gratitude and to keep electing incredible Democrats who will save our democracy from MAGA extremists like Donald Trump. »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/89042?t=9&akid=19531%2E878179%2E8HUZ1b) | [**Donate $3 to sign now »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/89042?t=10&akid=19531%2E878179%2E8HUZ1b)
---
**_X A grateful Democrat_**
\- The Voter Protection Project
| | Paid for by VPP
(www.protectvoting.org)
Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. 401 2nd Ave. S, Ste. 303, Seattle, WA 98104

The Voter Protection Project is on an important mission: To end partisan gerrymandering and voter suppression by electing candidates at the state and federal levels that are forceful advocates for voting rights. Our enfranchisement agenda includes working to ensure that every state has automatic voter registration and online voter registration, convenient early voting, and independent redistricting commissions to ensure politicians are not able to draw their own maps.

Please, consider making a [one-time contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/121?t=1&akid=19531%2E878179%2E8HUZ1b). **Or, to make the most powerful lasting impact and help us oust Republicans up and down the ballot who attack our right to vote, make a[monthly sustaining contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/122?t=2&akid=19531%2E878179%2E8HUZ1b).**

[Click here](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/fewer-emails/?t=3&akid=19531%2E878179%2E8HUZ1b) to request fewer emails. Click here to [unsubscribe](https://act.protectvoting.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=11&akid=19531%2E878179%2E8HUZ1b). | | [Please: Pitch In Once A Month To Protect Voting Rights »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/123?t=4&akid=19531%2E878179%2E8HUZ1b)
---
[Donate Just One Time »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/124?t=5&akid=19531%2E878179%2E8HUZ1b)

* **TOP NEWS**

"**NVIDIA eyes Arizona, Texas for $500 billion buildout of supercomputer infrastructure** ," via Reuters**** -- "Nvidia said it is planning to build AI infrastructure worth as much as $500 billion in the U.S. over the next four years with help from partners such as TSMC, the latest American tech firm to back the Trump administration's push for local manufacturing.

The announcement on Monday includes the production of its Blackwell AI chips at Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co's factory at Phoenix, Arizona.

Supercomputer manufacturing plants in Texas by Foxconn in Houston, and Wistron in Dallas, are expected to ramp up in the next 12 to 15 months, Nvidia said.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $7/mo](https://substack.com/redirect/a44952cc-6553-4ef3-bea6-17651daa31a8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
The move aligns the AI chip giant, majority of whose processors are produced in Taiwan, with a clutch of tech firms that have been pledging to bring manufacturing back to the U.S. amid the threat of steep tariffs from President **Donald Trump**.

Apple, which assembles most of its iPhones in China, has also promised [half a trillion dollars](https://substack.com/redirect/fccefb19-80bb-45a5-b86c-f46237a70233?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in the U.S. investments in the next four years including a factory in Texas for artificial intelligence servers.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $70/yr](https://substack.com/redirect/a44952cc-6553-4ef3-bea6-17651daa31a8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
"Adding American manufacturing helps us better meet the incredible and growing demand for AI chips and supercomputers, strengthens our supply chain and boosts our resiliency," said Nvidia CEO **Jensen Huan** g.

Manufacturing AI chips and supercomputers in the U.S. will create hundreds of thousands of jobs in the coming decades, the company said.

Huang had said in March Nvidia sees little short-term impact from higher U.S. tariffs, but would move production to the U.S. in the longer term, without giving a timeline.

Nvidia said on Monday TSMC has started production of its latest generation of chips at its factory in Arizona. Reuters reported in December TSMC was in talks with Nvidia to produce its Blackwell chips at the plant.

TSMC, the world's biggest contract maker of chips, has said it plans to make a fresh $100 billion investment in the U.S. that involves building five additional chip facilities." [Reuters](https://substack.com/redirect/06266b52-7b68-4473-9636-5878bd935565?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

The announcement on Monday includes the production of its Blackwell AI chips at Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co's factory at Phoenix, Arizona. Supercomputer manufacturing plants in Texas by Foxconn in Houston, and Wistron in Dallas, are expected to ramp up in the next 12 to 15 months, Nvidia said. The move aligns the AI chip giant, majority of whose processors are produced in Taiwan, with a clutch of tech firms that have been pledging to bring manufacturing back to the U.S. amid the threat of steep tariffs from President Donald Trump. Apple, which assembles most of its iPhones in China, has also promised in the U.S. investments in the next four years including a factory in Texas for artificial intelligence servers. "Adding American manufacturing helps us better meet the incredible and growing demand for AI chips and supercomputers, strengthens our supply chain and boosts our resiliency," said Nvidia CEO Jensen Huan g. Manufacturing AI chips and supercomputers in the U.S. will create hundreds of thousands of jobs in the coming decades, the company said. Huang had said in March Nvidia sees little short-term impact from higher U.S. tariffs, but would move production to the U.S. in the longer term, without giving a timeline. Nvidia said on Monday TSMC has started production of its latest generation of chips at its factory in Arizona. Reuters reported in December TSMC was in talks with Nvidia to produce its Blackwell chips at the plant. TSMC, the world's biggest contract maker of chips, has said it plans to make a fresh $100 billion investment in the U.S. that involves building five additional chip facilities." TOP NEWS "NVIDIA eyes Arizona, Texas for $500 billion buildout of supercomputer infrastructure ," via Reuters** -- "Nvidia said it is planning to build AI infrastructure worth as much as $500 billion in the U.S. over the next four years with help from partners such as TSMC, the latest American tech firm to back the Trump administration's push for local manufacturing. The announcement on Monday includes the production of its Blackwell AI chips at Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co's factory at Phoenix, Arizona. Supercomputer manufacturing plants in Texas by Foxconn in Houston, and Wistron in Dallas, are expected to ramp up in the next 12 to 15 months, Nvidia said. The move aligns the AI chip giant, majority of whose processors are produced in Taiwan, with a clutch of tech firms that have been pledging to bring manufacturing back to the U.S. amid the threat of steep tariffs from President Donald Trump. Apple, which assembles most of its iPhones in China, has also promised in the U.S. investments in the next four years including a factory in Texas for artificial intelligence servers. "Adding American manufacturing helps us better meet the incredible and growing demand for AI chips and supercomputers, strengthens our supply chain and boosts our resiliency," said Nvidia CEO Jensen Huan g. Manufacturing AI chips and supercomputers in the U.S. will create hundreds of thousands of jobs in the coming decades, the company said. Huang had said in March Nvidia sees little short-term impact from higher U.S. tariffs, but would move production to the U.S. in the longer term, without giving a timeline. Nvidia said on Monday TSMC has started production of its latest generation of chips at its factory in Arizona. Reuters reported in December TSMC was in talks with Nvidia to produce its Blackwell chips at the plant. TSMC, the world's biggest contract maker of chips, has said it plans to make a fresh $100 billion investment in the U.S. that involves building five additional chip facilities." * **TOP NEWS**

"**NVIDIA eyes Arizona, Texas for $500 billion buildout of supercomputer infrastructure** ,” via Reuters**** — “Nvidia said it is planning to build AI infrastructure worth as much as $500 billion in the U.S. over the next four years with help from partners such as TSMC, the latest American tech firm to back the Trump administration’s push for local manufacturing.

The announcement on Monday includes the production of its Blackwell AI chips at Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co’s factory at Phoenix, Arizona.

Supercomputer manufacturing plants in Texas by Foxconn in Houston, and Wistron in Dallas, are expected to ramp up in the next 12 to 15 months, Nvidia said.

“Adding American manufacturing helps us better meet the incredible and growing demand for AI chips and supercomputers, strengthens our supply chain and boosts our resiliency,” said Nvidia CEO **Jensen Huan** g.

Manufacturing AI chips and supercomputers in the U.S. will create hundreds of thousands of jobs in the coming decades, the company said.

Huang had said in March Nvidia sees little short-term impact from higher U.S. tariffs, but would move production to the U.S. in the longer term, without giving a timeline.

Nvidia said on Monday TSMC has started production of its latest generation of chips at its factory in Arizona. Reuters reported in December TSMC was in talks with Nvidia to produce its Blackwell chips at the plant.

TSMC, the world’s biggest contract maker of chips, has said it plans to make a fresh $100 billion investment in the U.S. that involves building five additional chip facilities." (limited time) | TOP NEWS "NVIDIA eyes Arizona, Texas for $500 billion buildout of supercomputer infrastructure ,” via Reuters** — “Nvidia said it is planning to build AI infrastructure worth as much as $500 billion in the U.S. over the next four years with help from partners such as TSMC, the latest American tech firm to back the Trump administration’s push for local manufacturing. The announcement on Monday includes the production of its Blackwell AI chips at Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co’s factory at Phoenix, Arizona. Supercomputer manufacturing plants in Texas by Foxconn in Houston, and Wistron in Dallas, are expected to ramp up in the next 12 to 15 months, Nvidia said. The move aligns the AI chip giant, majority of whose processors are produced in Taiwan, with a clutch of tech firms that have been pledging to bring manufacturing back to the U.S. amid the threat of steep tariffs from President Donald Trump. Apple, which assembles most of its iPhones in China, has also promised in the U.S. investments in the next four years including a factory in Texas for artificial intelligence servers. “Adding American manufacturing helps us better meet the incredible and growing demand for AI chips and supercomputers, strengthens our supply chain and boosts our resiliency,” said Nvidia CEO Jensen Huan g. Manufacturing AI chips and supercomputers in the U.S. will create hundreds of thousands of jobs in the coming decades, the company said. Huang had said in March Nvidia sees little short-term impact from higher U.S. tariffs, but would move production to the U.S. in the longer term, without giving a timeline. Nvidia said on Monday TSMC has started production of its latest generation of chips at its factory in Arizona. Reuters reported in December TSMC was in talks with Nvidia to produce its Blackwell chips at the plant. TSMC, the world’s biggest contract maker of chips, has said it plans to make a fresh $100 billion investment in the U.S. that involves building five additional chip facilities." | FREE SUBSCRIPTIONS HAVE ENDED : As of July 11, 2022, we have moved to a paywall for free subscribers.
